title: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
overview: Full-cost scholarship for outstanding international postgraduate students at the University of Cambridge (MPhil, PhD, one-year masters).
fundingDetails: University Composition Fee (tuition) paid; maintenance allowance £21,000 per 12 months (2024–25 rate); one economy airfare at start and end; visa & IHS costs covered; discretionary academic development funding available.
countries: United Kingdom (University of Cambridge)
institutions: University of Cambridge — example eligible programmes: (1) MPhil in Engineering, (2) MPhil in Finance, (3) MPhil in Public Policy, (4) MPhil in Development Studies, (5) PhD in Biological Sciences, (6) PhD in Computer Science. Direct list of Cambridge postgraduate courses: https://www.postgraduate.study.cam.ac.uk/courses
requirements:
Documents required (per Gates Cambridge application pages & Cambridge Graduate Admissions):
- Completed University of Cambridge Graduate Application form (include funding section for Gates)
- Degree certificate(s) (Bachelors and Masters where applicable) — official copies
- Full academic transcripts from all tertiary institutions (sealed where required)
- Research proposal (PhD) or statement of purpose (masters)
- Curriculum vitae (CV)
- Academic references: at least three references (submitted via the application portal)
- English language test score if required by department (IELTS/TOEFL) — see course page
- Passport/national ID copy
(Official source: Gates Cambridge how-to-apply & eligibility pages)
applicationCycle: Applications open in September each year; Gates operates two rounds (US and International). See timeline: https://www.gatescambridge.org/apply/timeline/
notes: Applicants must apply for admission to a Cambridge course AND complete the Gates funding section; departmental nomination is part of selection.

title: Rhodes Scholarship
overview: Fully-funded postgraduate scholarship to study at the University of Oxford (DPhil/masters). Merit-based, leadership & service emphasis.
fundingDetails: Covers University of Oxford course fees; annual stipend £20,400 per year (2025–26 rate); return economy flights; visa/IHS and settling-in allowances.
countries: United Kingdom (University of Oxford)
institutions: University of Oxford — example programmes (1) DPhil in Medicine, (2) MSc in Financial Economics, (3) MSt in Social Science of the Internet, (4) MSc in Environmental Change and Management, (5) DPhil in History, (6) MSc in Computer Science. Oxford course directory: https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/courses
requirements:
Per Rhodes global guidance and constituency pages (key required documents):
- Rhodes online application form ( constituency route as applicable )
- Official transcripts for all tertiary study
- Degree certificate or proof of expected graduation
- Two or three letters of recommendation (number depends on constituency)
- Personal statement / statement of purpose
- CV (including leadership & service experience)
- Proof of citizenship / passport
- English language evidence if required by Oxford department
- Additional constituency-specific documents (nomination forms) — see your constituency page
(See Rhodes guidance PDF: global nominations guidance and constituency pages)
applicationCycle: Nominations and applications open (example 2 June 2025) and final nominations deadline 31 July 2025 for 2026 cycle; constituency-specific deadlines apply. See: https://www.rhodeshouse.ox.ac.uk/scholarships/applications/
notes: Selection via constituency; some constituencies require institutional nomination; check local Rhodes secretary or national body.

title: MEXT (Japanese Government) Scholarship — Research Students
overview: MEXT scholarships for international students to study in Japan (research students: Master and PhD levels).
fundingDetails: Master’s: ¥144,000 per month; Doctoral: ¥145,000 per month (MEXT published rates); tuition & entrance fees usually waived; one round-trip ticket to Japan.
countries: Japan (at Japanese universities that accept MEXT grantees)
institutions: Participating Japanese universities (examples often host MEXT scholars): (1) University of Tokyo, (2) Kyoto University, (3) Osaka University, (4) Tohoku University, (5) Nagoya University, (6) Hokkaido University. For host lists and placement info see MEXT application guidelines: https://www.mext.go.jp/en/policy/education/highered/title02/detail02/sdetail02/
requirements:
Documents required (from MEXT application guidelines PDF):
- Application form (official MEXT form)
- Official transcripts from all tertiary institutions (sealed/issued by registrar)
- Certificate of graduation or prospective graduation (BSc certificate for masters applicants)
- Research plan (for research students)
- Letters of recommendation (as specified in embassy/university call)
- Medical certificate (health check form)
- Passport copy
- Letter of reference / supervisor support for university‑recommended track (if applicable)
- Japanese/English language evidence if requested by host
(Reference: MEXT application guideline PDFs linked below)
applicationCycle: Annual embassy nomination & university recommended tracks — deadlines differ by embassy; check MEXT embassy call PDF for your country (e.g., application windows often in April–June for October/April placement).
notes: Some tracks (research students) require a letter of acceptance or placement preference; documents often must be submitted in sealed envelopes to embassy.

title: Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC)
overview: China Scholarship Council (CSC) scholarships for international Masters/PhD students in China.
fundingDetails: Tuition waiver; accommodation sometimes provided; monthly living allowance varies by host and category (examples: CNY 3,000/month Masters; CNY 3,500/month PhD) — check host-university CSC pages for exact amounts.
countries: China (at participating Chinese universities)
institutions: Designated host universities include (examples): (1) Peking University, (2) Tsinghua University, (3) Fudan University, (4) Shanghai Jiao Tong University, (5) Zhejiang University, (6) Nanjing University. For designated university lists see CSC/host university pages: https://www.chinesescholarshipcouncil.com/
requirements:
CSC application documentation (depending on category) typically includes:
- CSC application form (online / host university upload)
- Bachelor/Master degree certificate (copy) or proof of expected graduation
- Official transcripts (sealed) for all tertiary study
- Study/research plan or research proposal (PhD applicants)
- Recommendation letters (number depends on host/university; common: 2)
- Passport copy
- Physical examination record (medical certificate) where requested
- Language proficiency evidence if required by program
(See CSC portal and host university CSC pages for category-specific checklists)
applicationCycle: CSC central application windows vary by category (Spring/August intakes); most host universities accept CSC applicants in early year—check CSC portal and host university designated program page for exact dates.
notes: There are several CSC scholarship categories (bilateral, university-specific, CSC scholarship for general students); read the category call.

title: Eiffel Excellence Scholarship (France)
overview: French government scholarship managed by Campus France for top international Master’s and PhD candidates nominated by French institutions.
fundingDetails: Official monthly allowances (published): Master’s approx €1,181/month; Doctoral approx €1,700–€1,800/month; Eiffel generally covers living allowance and certain services; tuition usually not covered.
countries: France (at French HEIs nominating candidates)
institutions: Examples of French institutions that nominate candidates: (1) Université PSL, (2) Sorbonne University, (3) École Polytechnique, (4) Institut Polytechnique de Paris, (5) Sciences Po, (6) Université Paris-Saclay. For institutional nomination guidance see Campus France Eiffel page: https://www.campusfrance.org/system/files/medias/documents/2024-12/REGULATIONS_EIFFEL_PROGRAM_2025_EN.pdf
requirements:
From Campus France/Regulations (applicants must be nominated by a French HEI; documents typically required by nominating institution include):
- Application form (submitted by nominating institution)
- CV and motivation letter (sometimes specified length by institution)
- Copies of degree certificates and transcripts (translated to French/English if necessary)
- Proof of enrolment/offer from nominating institution (institution nominates candidates)
- Passport copy
- Any programme-specific documents (research proposal for doctoral candidates)
(Exact checklist supplied by nominating French HEI; Campus France is the administrative hub.)
applicationCycle: Institutions nominate candidates annually per call (example: Eiffel 2026 call open until 8 Jan 2026); check Campus France call page.
notes: Eiffel is not directly applied to by students; French HEIs submit nominations via Campus France.

title: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships
overview: Swiss federal scholarships for international researchers (PhD/postdoc) for study/research stays at Swiss HEIs.
fundingDetails: Research/PhD fellowship: CHF 1,920 per month (recent SBFI published rate); additional housing/insurance contributions may apply.
countries: Switzerland (at Swiss universities & research institutes)
institutions: Participating institutions include (examples): (1) ETH Zurich, (2) EPFL, (3) University of Zurich, (4) University of Geneva, (5) University of Bern, (6) University of Basel. SBFI country pages list recognized institutions: https://www.sbfi.admin.ch/en/swiss-government-excellence-scholarships
requirements:
SBFI/Swiss Government application requirements typically include:
- Detailed CV
- Letter of motivation/cover letter
- 2-3 letters of recommendation
- Research plan (2–3 pages) with timeline
- Copies of degrees and transcripts (Master’s or equivalent for PhD grants)
- Letter of support from a host supervisor in Switzerland (often required)
- Passport copy and other ID
(See SBFI FAQ/apply pages for precise document templates and country submission rules)
applicationCycle: Annual call; application windows and country-specific deadlines published on SBFI country pages (calls often open in August/Sept).
notes: Applicants usually apply via Swiss embassy or national commission; some countries accept direct online application.

title: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degrees (EMJMD)
overview: EU-funded joint Master’s programmes (consortia of European universities) offering scholarships to top applicants worldwide.
fundingDetails: Unit scholarship €1,400 per month (EU rate) + tuition waiver + travel & installation contributions; total depends on programme length (e.g., up to €33,600 for 24 months).
countries: Consortium partner countries in Europe (students study across 2+ countries per programme)
institutions: EMJMD comprises many specific consortia; example partner universities from different consortia: (1) University of Barcelona, (2) University of Bologna, (3) KU Leuven, (4) University of Edinburgh, (5) TU Delft, (6) University of Warsaw. Search catalogue of EMJMD programmes: https://erasmus-plus.ec.europa.eu/opportunities/opportunities-for-individuals/students/erasmus-mundus-joint-masters
requirements:
Typical EMJMD applicant documents (per Erasmus+ programme guidance and individual consortium pages):
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent (degree certificate)
- Official academic transcripts
- CV/Resume
- Motivation letter / statement of purpose
- Proof of language proficiency (English or other as required)
- Letters of recommendation (usually 2)
- Specific consortium documents (e.g., portfolio for arts programmes)
- Passport copy
(Each consortium publishes precise document list and application form on its programme page.)
applicationCycle: Application windows set by each consortium (commonly closed in Jan–Apr for autumn intake); check individual programme pages in the EMJMD catalogue.
notes: Consortia manage selection and may set additional eligibility conditions.

title: Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals (SI)
overview: Full scholarships for selected Master’s programmes in Sweden targeting future global professionals.
fundingDetails: Full tuition fee coverage + monthly allowance SEK 12,000 + one-time travel grant SEK 15,000 (as published by SI).
countries: Sweden (at participating Swedish universities and eligible master’s programmes)
institutions: Participating Swedish universities & sample programmes: (1) Lund University - MSc programmes, (2) KTH Royal Institute of Technology - MSc programmes, (3) Uppsala University - MSc programmes, (4) Stockholm University - MSc programs, (5) Umeå University - MSc programs, (6) University of Gothenburg - MSc programs. SI publishes eligible programmes: https://si.se/en/apply/scholarships/swedish-institute-scholarships-for-global-professionals/ and SI portal https://apply-scholarships.si.se/
requirements:
SI application required documents (as per SI call and application portal):
- Completed SI online application
- Application to an eligible Master’s programme (University application confirmation or offer may be required)
- Passport copy
- Curriculum Vitae (CV)
- Personal statement/motivation letter (per SI portal instructions)
- Proof of work/leadership experience (if applicable)
- Two references (contact details and/or letters)
- Degree certificates and transcripts (translated to English if necessary)
- Proof of English language where required
(See SI portal for exact page limits and required fields)
applicationCycle: SI application window generally opens in late autumn and closes in Jan–Feb for autumn intake; check SI call page for exact dates.
notes: Applicants can only submit one SI application; selection emphasizes leadership and potential to contribute to development.

title: KAUST Fellowship
overview: University fellowship automatically offered to admitted graduate students at King Abdullah University of Science & Technology (MS & PhD).
fundingDetails: Full tuition + stipend (MS ~USD 20,000/yr; PhD ~USD 25,000–30,000/yr) + on-campus housing, health insurance, relocation support (see KAUST fees & funding).
countries: Saudi Arabia (KAUST campus in Thuwal)
institutions: KAUST graduate programs (examples): (1) MS in Computer Science, (2) MS in Electrical Engineering, (3) MS in Environmental Science, (4) PhD in Chemical Engineering, (5) PhD in Biological and Environmental Science, (6) PhD in Applied Mathematics. Program list: https://admissions.kaust.edu.sa/programs
requirements:
KAUST graduate application requirements (from KAUST admissions pages):
- Online application form
- Official complete university transcripts and graduation certificates (scanned + certified English translations)
- CV/Resume
- Statement of purpose / research interests
- Two or three letters of recommendation
- English language test scores if required (TOEFL/IELTS)
- Passport copy
- Additional program-specific documents (e.g., GRE, portfolio) where requested
(See KAUST admission requirements page)
applicationCycle: KAUST uses rolling admissions with program deadlines listed per intake on KAUST admissions pages.
notes: Fellowship automatically awarded to admitted students in funded programs; check the MSc/PhD program page for deadlines.

title: NUS Research Scholarship (NUSRS)
overview: Research scholarships for full-time research Master’s and PhD students at the National University of Singapore.
fundingDetails: Full tuition waiver + monthly stipend: International PhD S$3,000/month; International Master’s research S$2,900/month (as published by NUS).
countries: Singapore (NUS)
institutions: NUS research degree programs (examples): (1) PhD in Computer Science, (2) PhD in Physics, (3) MSc(Research) in Environmental Studies, (4) PhD in Biomedical Sciences, (5) PhD in Engineering, (6) PhD in Social Sciences. NUS graduate programme list: https://www.nus.edu.sg/graduate-studies
requirements:
Typical NUS Research Scholarship documents (per NUS Graduate Studies and scholarship pages):
- Completed NUS graduate application form
- Official transcripts and degree certificates
- Research proposal (for research masters/PhD)
- CV/Resume
- Two or three academic references
- Proof of English language proficiency (if required)
- Passport copy
(Selection handled by departments; scholarship offered on admission)
applicationCycle: Scholarships are considered with admission; check NUS admissions deadlines by faculty.
notes: Departments may award top-up or additional allowances; scholarship terms on NUS pages.

title: Clarendon Fund Scholarships
overview: Fully-funded scholarships for outstanding graduate applicants to the University of Oxford (covers fees + living costs).
fundingDetails: Full tuition & college fees covered + an annual maintenance grant (Clarendon maintenance ≥ £18,622/year in recent years; check Clarendon pages for current year).
countries: United Kingdom (University of Oxford)
institutions: University of Oxford — example programmes: (1) DPhil in Clinical Medicine, (2) MSc in Economics for Development, (3) MSc in Computer Science, (4) MPhil in Political Theory, (5) DPhil in Materials, (6) MSc in Evidence-based Social Intervention. Oxford course directory: https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/courses
requirements:
Clarendon selection comes from the pool of applicants. Required application documents for Oxford admission include:
- Graduate application form to Oxford
- Degree certificates and full academic transcripts
- Research proposal (for DPhil) or statement of purpose
- CV/Resume
- Academic references (usually 2-3)
- English language evidence if required
(Clarendon does not require a separate application; see Oxford graduate admissions checklist)
applicationCycle: Apply to Oxford by the course deadline (varies by course); Clarendon selects from eligible applicants after admissions decisions.
notes: Clarendon offers around 100+ scholarships annually (check current figures on Clarendon site).

title: Commonwealth Scholarships (CSC)
overview: Multiple scholarship schemes for citizens of eligible Commonwealth countries to study in the UK (Masters, PhD, split-site, distance learning).
fundingDetails: Covers approved tuition fees; return airfare; stipend/living allowance (see CSC allowances table); study travel grants and other approved costs per scheme.
countries: United Kingdom (for study in UK institutions)
institutions: Participating UK universities that commonly host Commonwealth scholars (examples): (1) University of Edinburgh, (2) University of Manchester, (3) University of Leeds, (4) King's College London, (5) University of Warwick, (6) London School of Economics (participating institutions change by scheme and year). CSC scheme central page: https://cscuk.fcdo.gov.uk/
requirements:
CSC Central scheme application documents generally include:
- Completed CSC online application via CSC Central
- Passport/birth certificate (proof of nationality)
- Degree certificates and full transcripts
- Two referee reports (details per scheme)
- Personal statement / study proposal (word limits specified on CSC forms)
- Admission letter (where required)
- Evidence of professional experience (for some schemes)
- English language certificate where required
(Exact documentation and word limits shown on CSC application system and scheme handbooks)
applicationCycle: Schemes open annually; country-specific deadlines and nominating agency deadlines apply. Check CSC central pages and country pages for exact dates.
notes: Multiple variants (Shared, Split-site, Distance Learning); each variant has its own handbook.

title: Government of Ireland - International Education Scholarships (GOI-IES)
overview: Irish government scholarship for international students (one-year Masters) at participating Irish HEIs.
fundingDetails: Full tuition fee waiver + one-year stipend €10,000 (paid in two instalments).
countries: Ireland (selected higher education institutions)
institutions: Participating Irish HEIs (examples): (1) Trinity College Dublin, (2) University College Dublin, (3) University College Cork, (4) National University of Ireland Galway, (5) University of Limerick, (6) Dublin City University. HEA GOI-IES participating HEIs list in call document: https://hea.ie/policy/internationalisation/goi-ies/
requirements:
From the GOI-IES call document (2026 call PDF includes checklist):
- Completed online application via GOI-IES portal
- Proof of offer/conditional offer from an eligible Irish HEI (some calls require applicants to have admission)
- Degree certificates and transcripts
- Two referee details (and references as required)
- Personal statement (word/char limits specified in call)
- Passport copy
- Any additional documents requested by host HEI
(See 2026 GOI-IES call document for exact word limits and appendix checklists)
applicationCycle: Annual call; 2026 call example: application portal open with deadlines specified in call (check HEA GOI-IES page).
notes: Only one application per candidate allowed; HEA administrates the scheme.

title: DAAD Scholarships (various programmes)
overview: German Academic Exchange Service offers many scholarships for international postgraduate students, researchers and development-related courses.
fundingDetails: Common DAAD scholarship example: monthly stipend ~€992/month for many postgraduate scholarships; doctoral lines often higher (~€1,200–€1,500); includes travel allowance and insurance per call.
countries: Germany (at German HEIs)
institutions: DAAD funds numerous German institutions; example partner universities: (1) Humboldt University Berlin, (2) Technical University of Munich, (3) Free University of Berlin, (4) RWTH Aachen University, (5) University of Freiburg, (6) University of Bonn. Use DAAD scholarship database to find program-partner lists: https://www.daad.de/en/study-and-research-in-germany/scholarships-database/
requirements:
DAAD application common documents (per DAAD scholarship database and calls):
- Completed DAAD online application form
- Curriculum vitae (CV)
- Letter of motivation / study plan
- Academic transcripts and degree certificates
- Two letters of recommendation
- Language competency proof (German/English as required)
- Research proposal (for doctoral applicants)
- Passport copy
(Check specific DAAD programme call page for exact word limits and templates)
applicationCycle: Each DAAD programme has its own timeline; many calls open in autumn/winter for next intake; see DAAD call page for deadlines.
notes: DAAD offers EPOS, Helmut-Schmidt, In-Country/In-Region scholarships with differing eligibility.

title: Türkiye Scholarships (Türkiye Bursları)
overview: Turkish government scholarships for international students for full-degree study in Turkey (BSc/MSc/PhD).
fundingDetails: Tuition fees, monthly stipend (varies by level), accommodation or housing allowance, health insurance, and travel costs (detailed on portal).
countries: Turkey (at Turkish universities)
institutions: Participating Turkish HEIs (examples): (1) Middle East Technical University (METU), (2) Boğaziçi University, (3) Istanbul Technical University, (4) Hacettepe University, (5) Koç University, (6) Sabancı University. Portal lists eligible universities and programs: https://www.turkiyeburslari.gov.tr/
requirements:
Per Türkiye Scholarships portal 'Apply steps' required documents include:
- Valid passport/national ID
- Recent passport photo
- Diploma or temporary graduation certificate (or proof of expected graduation)
- Academic transcripts
- Letter of intent/personal statement
- Two letters of recommendation
- Research proposal for PhD applicants
- Language test results where required (TOEFL/IELTS/GRE as per program)
(See official 'Apply Steps' for upload checklist)
applicationCycle: Annual application window (commonly January–February); check portal for exact dates.
notes: Eligibility includes age limits by degree level; preparatory Turkish language courses may be required.

title: VLIR-UOS Scholarships (MasterMind)
overview: Flemish (Belgium) development scholarships (MasterMind and other programmes) for candidates from eligible countries to pursue selected development-oriented Master’s in Flanders.
fundingDetails: Covers tuition, monthly living allowance (exact amount per call), travel, accommodation and insurance (see VLIR-UOS call).
countries: Belgium (Flanders universities)
institutions: Participating Flemish universities and programmes (examples): (1) KU Leuven partner programmes, (2) Universiteit Gent partner masters, (3) University of Antwerp partner masters, (4) Universiteit Hasselt partner programmes, (5) University Colleges Leuven-Limburg collaborations, (6) Vrije Universiteit Brussel partner programmes. Programme list & calls: https://www.vliruos.be/en/scholarships
requirements:
VLIR-UOS MasterMind application documents typically include:
- Completed online application form per programme
- Bachelor’s degree certificate and transcripts
- Proof of professional experience (for some tracks)
- Curriculum vitae (CV)
- Motivation letter / study plan
- Two references or referee contact details
- Passport copy
(Program-specific documents and eligibility published in each call)
applicationCycle: Calls published annually with programme-specific deadlines; check VLIR-UOS call page for dates.
notes: Focus on development-oriented training linked to partner country needs.

title: Stipendium Hungaricum
overview: Hungarian government scholarship for international students to pursue full-degree studies in Hungary.
fundingDetails: Tuition waiver + monthly stipend (amounts published per intake, example HUF 43,700/month reported in past calls), plus possible contribution to accommodation.
countries: Hungary (participating Hungarian universities)
institutions: Participating Hungarian HEIs include (examples): (1) Eötvös Loránd University (ELTE), (2) University of Debrecen, (3) Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME), (4) University of Szeged, (5) Corvinus University of Budapest, (6) University of Pécs. Full list and program catalog: https://stipendiumhungaricum.hu/
requirements:
Stipendium Hungaricum application documents typically include:
- Online application via the portal
- Copy of passport
- Degree certificates and transcripts (and translations where necessary)
- Motivation letter
- Research proposal (for PhD applicants)
- Two references or contact details for referees
- Language proof where required
(See Stipendium Hungaricum portal for exact upload checklist)
applicationCycle: Annual call (usually opens in November/December for the next academic year); check portal for dates.
notes: Scholarship duration equals programme length; some programmes include airport pickup.

title: ETH Zurich — Excellence Scholarships & Opportunity Programme (ESOP)
overview: ETH Zurich scholarships supporting outstanding international Master’s students (covers tuition and living contribution).
fundingDetails: Covers statutory tuition costs + living contribution; exact amount varies by scholarship and year (check ESOP/ETH scholarship pages for current figures).
countries: Switzerland (ETH Zurich)
institutions: ETH Master's programmes (examples): (1) MSc in Mechanical Engineering, (2) MSc in Architecture, (3) MSc in Management Technology, (4) MSc in Data Science, (5) MSc in Physics, (6) MSc in Civil Engineering. ETH program list: https://www.ethz.ch/en/studies/master.html
requirements:
ETH ESOP / Excellence scholarship requirements typically include:
- Admission application to an ETH Master’s programme
- Completed scholarship application (ESOP) where required
- Academic transcripts and degree certificates
- Letters of recommendation (number per ETH call)
- CV/Resume
- Personal statement or motivation letter
- Proof of language proficiency where required
(Exact ESOP call instructions on ETH scholarships page)
applicationCycle: ETH Master’s admissions and scholarship deadlines vary by program; ESOP aligns with ETH admissions (usually Dec/Jan for fall intake).
notes: ETH also has departmental scholarships and external funding options.

title: Australia Awards Scholarships
overview: Australian government scholarships for students from eligible countries to study in Australia in development-priority fields.
fundingDetails: Full tuition fees; return economy airfares; establishment allowance; fortnightly contribution to living expenses; OSHC; possible fieldwork allowances (full entitlements published in DFAT policy).
countries: Australia (at participating Australian HEIs)
institutions: Participating Australian institutions commonly include (examples): (1) Australian National University, (2) University of Melbourne, (3) University of Sydney, (4) Monash University, (5) University of Queensland, (6) University of New South Wales. Country program pages list eligible institutions by country.
requirements:
Australia Awards application documents generally include:
- Completed online application via the Australia Awards portal for your country
- Academic transcripts and degree certificates
- CV/Resume
- Personal statement / development impact statement
- Proof of English language (if required)
- Passport copy
- Employer references or proof of service (where required)
(See DFAT Australia Awards country pages for exact checklist)
applicationCycle: Country-specific application windows (published by DFAT and Australian embassies each year).
notes: Awards include a return service obligation to the home country (encouraged to return and contribute).

title: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships (Vanier CGS)
overview: Prestigious Canadian doctoral scholarship to attract world-class PhD students to Canadian institutions; administered by the Tri-agencies (CIHR/NSERC/SSHRC). Institution nomination required.
fundingDetails: CAD $50,000 per 12 months; duration: up to 3 years. Source: Vanier official 'Value' statement.
countries: Canada (host country).
institutions: Examples: University of Toronto; University of British Columbia; McGill University; University of Alberta; McMaster University; University of Ottawa. Official allocations/list: https://vanier.gc.ca/en/allocations_by_university-liste_d_attributions.html
requirements:
Verbatim excerpt (Vanier Information for Nominating Institutions):
"Nomination package must include: completed nomination form; nomination letter; CV; research proposal; transcripts; two letters of reference; other supporting documents as required by nominating institution." See full guidance: https://vanier.gc.ca/en/nomination_process-processus_de_mise_en_candidature.html

Vanier CGS - Application/nomination checklist (verbatim excerpts from Vanier official pages):

• ResearchNet application form (includes two letters of reference, each with three sections).
• CCV (Common CV) (limited entries; see Completing the CCV for complete information).
• Research contributions (maximum one page).
• Personal leadership statement (maximum 2 pages for English applications; maximum 2.5 pages for French applications).
• Special circumstances (maximum 1.0 page, optional).
• Two leadership letters of reference (maximum 2 pages each).
• Official transcripts (nominating institution must upload up-to-date transcript; summary of international transcripts optional).
• Institutional nomination form / nomination letter (as per nominating institution guidance).

Source: https://vanier.gc.ca/en/nomination_process-processus_de_mise_en_candidature.html ; https://vanier.gc.ca/en/information_for_institutions-information_pour_etablissements.html
applicationCycle: Institutional nomination deadlines vary; national nomination submission date to Vanier typically in late Oct annually (see institutional guidance). Example: check nominating institution page for exact date. (Vanier pages).
notes: Up to 166 new awards annually; nomination by eligible Canadian institution only; award non-transferable; supplementary institutional funding possible.

title: Global Korea Scholarship (GKS / KGSP) - NIIED
overview: South Korean government scholarship (NIIED) for undergraduate & graduate international students to study in Korea via Embassy or University track; includes language training.
fundingDetails: Components vary by track/university. Typical published items: full tuition (varies by university); monthly living allowance (examples published in university/GKS notices — NOT A SINGLE GLOBAL FIGURE). Example amounts vary (e.g., some notices show KRW 1,133,330/month for certain categories).
countries: Republic of Korea (host country).
institutions: Examples: Seoul National University; Korea University; Yonsei University; KAIST; POSTECH; Ewha Womans University. Official GKS notice and university lists: https://www.studyinkorea.go.kr/en/main.do
requirements:
Excerpt from Study in Korea (GKS guideline PDF):
"Required Documents: Application form; Personal statement; Study plan/Research proposal; Recommendation letters; Graduation certificate (or proof of expected graduation); Academic transcripts; Passport copy; Language certificates (if required); Other documents as specified by embassy/university." Full PDF: see Study in Korea GKS Notice.

GKS (NIIED) - Documentation Requirements (verbatim excerpts from GKS Application Guidelines PDF):

Documents to complete: Application form, Personal Statement, Study Plan, Research Proposal, Letter of Recommendation, Letter of Invitation, Applicant Agreement, Personal Medical Assessment, Consent to Collect and Use Personal Information (Forms 1~10).

Required certificates: Graduation certificate, academic transcript, proof of citizenship, proof of overseas Koreans, etc.

Documents to be submitted by Evaluation Stage:
 - Embassy/University (first round): submission requirements set by embassy/university.
 - NIIED (second round): All forms must be filled in English or Korean with applicant's original handwritten signature. Embassy Track: ONE set of original documents + THREE sets of additional simple photocopies. University Track: ONE set of original documents.

Specific required documents (verbatim items from GKS Guidelines):
11 Applicant's/Parent(s)' Proof of Citizenship and family relationship Document (Required)
12 Bachelor's Graduation Certificate (or Diploma) (Required)
12' Bachelor's Degree Transcript (Required)
13 Master's Graduation Certificate (or Diploma) (Required, if applicable)
13' Master's Degree Transcript (Required, if applicable)
Doctoral Graduation Certificate/Transcript (if applicable)
Letter of Recommendation(s) — must be signed and sealed; dated within one year of application.

Notes: All application materials submitted to NIIED WILL NOT BE RETURNED IN ANY CASE. Documents in languages other than English/Korean must be accompanied by certified translations and apostille/consular confirmation as required.

Source: https://gksscholarship.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/2025-GKS-G-Application-Guidelines-English.pdf
applicationCycle: Application windows published annually (Embassy track typically Sep; University track Sep–Oct). See official GKS notice for exact dates: https://www.studyinkorea.go.kr
notes: Two tracks (Embassy & University); language training usually precedes degree; stipend payments subject to bank/account rules.

title: Fulbright Foreign Student Program (USA)
overview: U.S. government-funded exchange program enabling graduate students, young professionals and artists from around the world to study in the U.S.; administered by U.S. embassies and Fulbright Commissions.
fundingDetails: NOT PUBLISHED centrally — components commonly include tuition, living stipend, round-trip airfare, and health insurance. Exact amounts set by each Fulbright country program; check country Fulbright page for numeric values.
countries: United States (host country).
institutions: Examples of U.S. host universities receiving Fulbrighters: Harvard University; Columbia University; UC Berkeley; University of Chicago; Yale University; MIT. Fulbright placements vary annually. More: https://foreign.fulbrightonline.org/
requirements:
From Fulbright Foreign Student 'How to Apply' (official):
"Typical components: online application via your country Fulbright Commission or US Embassy; academic transcripts; statement of grant purpose/study-research statement; 2-3 letters of recommendation; CV/resume; copies of diplomas; English language test scores if required; country-specific documents. See your country page for exact checklist."

Fulbright Foreign Student Program - How to Apply (verbatim excerpts from Fulbright global 'How to Apply'):

• Apply through the Fulbright Commission/Foundation or U.S. Embassy in your home country. Country-specific application guidelines and required documents vary; see your country page.
• Typical application components include: Application form submitted to Fulbright office; Academic transcripts; Diplomas/degrees; Letters of recommendation; Statement of purpose/Study plan/Research proposal; Language test scores (if required); Standardized test scores (e.g., GRE) where specified by country or host institution; Medical clearance and passport copy as required by country office.
• Eligibility: reside in country of nomination at time of application; hold the equivalent of a U.S. bachelor's degree by program start; demonstrate English fluency (recommended TOEFL/IELTS minimums noted on country pages).

Source (country-specific details & checklists): https://foreign.fulbrightonline.org/apply  (please use your home-country Fulbright page for the verbatim country checklist)
applicationCycle: Country-specific; national deadlines published on each Fulbright country page. Example: check your local U.S. embassy/Fulbright Commission page.
notes: Approximately 4,000 awards/year globally; selection and benefits vary by country; grantees must follow visa/health insurance rules of the U.S.

title: Chevening Scholarships (UK)
overview: UK government global scholarship scheme (FCDO-backed) providing full funding for selected one-year master's degree programmes in the UK to future leaders.
fundingDetails: Chevening covers tuition fees, a monthly personal living allowance (variable, not centrally published), an arrival allowance, homeward departure allowance, the cost of one visa application, and a travel grant to Chevening events. Exact stipend amounts are not published centrally. See Chevening finance pages.
countries: United Kingdom (host country).
institutions: Eligible courses listed via Chevening course finder; examples: University of Oxford (qualifying master's), LSE, UCL, King's College London, University of Edinburgh, University of Manchester. Course finder: https://www.chevening.org/apply/find-a-course/
requirements:
From Chevening guidance (verbatim excerpts):
"You will be asked to provide: two referees (names and contacts) and, if invited, two reference letters; an undergraduate degree certificate (official document proving degree and grade); evidence of work experience; passport; any required English language test scores if requested by country office." Full checklist: https://www.chevening.org/resource-hub/guidance/prepare-your-application/

Chevening Scholarships - 'Prepare your application' (verbatim checklist excerpts):

From Chevening guidance pages: applicants will be asked to provide the following during the online application:
• Passport details.
• Education: degree details, transcripts.
• Employment details: positions, employer contact.
• Two references: names and contact details (referees later submit references directly).
• Personal statements / essays: Career plan (500 words), Leadership and influence (500 words), Networking (500 words) — see application for exact word limits each cycle.
• Supporting documents: degree certificates, transcripts, English language evidence (if required), work references and CV as specified.

Chevening publishes exact required attachments per cycle on the official site; please consult the official 'Prepare your application' and the 'Supporting documents' pages for the verbatim checklist.

Source: https://www.chevening.org/resource-hub/guidance/prepare-your-application/
applicationCycle: Annual global application window (dates vary by year). Deadlines posted on Chevening site and country pages.
notes: Minimum two years' work experience, citizenship requirements by country, ineligible to apply if already have degree from UK, must apply to three eligible UK courses.

title: Australian Government — Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarships (Australia)
overview: Australian Government RTP provides funding support (tuition offsets and stipends) for domestic and international research higher degree students through universities' allocation.
fundingDetails: Full-time base RTP stipend rate (2026 example): AUD $39,069 per annum (as published on university pages like ANU and some universities use $39,069 or similar). Tuition offsets vary (full or partial tuition as published per university).
countries: Study in Australia at participating universities.
institutions: Examples: Australian National University; University of Melbourne; University of Sydney; University of Queensland; Monash University; University of Adelaide. Universities administer RTP scholarships locally.
requirements:
Typical RTP scholarship application checklist (university example, ANU):
"Application form; academic transcripts; proof of degree/diploma; CV; research proposal; referee reports; proof of English proficiency (if applicable); other program-specific documents." See university RTP pages for full checklist.

Australian Government Research Training Program (RTP) - typical institutional application checklist (example excerpts from ANU / Australian Govt pages):

Note: RTP scholarships are administered by each Australian university. Example (ANU) required materials:
• Online application to the university (no separate RTP central application).
• Academic transcripts and degree certificates (official copies as specified).
• Research proposal / research plan (length as per faculty guidance).
• CV / resume.
• References / referee contacts (often two or three referees; referees may be emailed a reference link or asked to send reference letters directly to the university).
• Proof of English language proficiency (IELTS/TOEFL) if applicable.
• Any program-specific requirements (e.g., pre-enrolment approvals, supervisor support letter).

Source (example authoritative institutional): https://study.anu.edu.au/scholarships/find-scholarship/australian-government-research-training-program-rtp-stipend and Dept. Education RTP pages.
applicationCycle: Deadlines vary by university and intake; universities publish dates (e.g., ANU/UniMelb scholarship pages).
notes: Duration typically 3–4 years for PhD; stipend indexed annually; extension possibilities for parental leave; conditions differ per university.

title: Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships (University of Melbourne)
overview: University of Melbourne's Graduate Research Scholarships provide living allowances and fee offsets for graduate research (Masters by Research and PhD) students.
fundingDetails: Living allowance of AUD $39,500 per year pro rata (2026 full-time rate) for up to 2 years (Masters by Research) or up to 3.5–4 years for PhD; full fee offset for eligible periods.
countries: Study in Australia (University of Melbourne).
institutions: University of Melbourne faculties and research programs across engineering, science, business, medicine, arts, etc. (example: Faculty of Engineering, Faculty of Medicine, etc.). Official page lists eligibility and programs.
requirements:
From University of Melbourne Scholarships page (excerpt):
"Applicants must provide academic transcripts; research proposal; CV; referee contact details; evidence of English proficiency where required; any additional program-specific documents." Full checklist: https://scholarships.unimelb.edu.au/awards/graduate-research-scholarships

Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships - Required application documents (verbatim from University of Melbourne scholarship pages):

• International applicants: apply for admission to a graduate research degree and indicate scholarship interest.
• Supporting documents: academic transcripts and degree certificates, curriculum vitae, research proposal (as required by faculty), evidence of English proficiency (IELTS/TOEFL) where applicable, referee details (usually two academic referees).
• Additional items as listed on the official Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships page and each faculty's scholarship instructions.

Source: https://scholarships.unimelb.edu.au/awards/melbourne-graduate-research-scholarship  (see 'How to apply' and 'Supporting documents')
applicationCycle: Application deadlines and intakes published on University of Melbourne research scholarships page; varies by faculty.
notes: Includes limited paid sick, maternity and parenting leave; living allowance indexed; duration varies by degree.

title: University of Otago Doctoral Scholarship (New Zealand)
overview: University of Otago doctoral scholarship providing stipend and fees waiver for international and domestic PhD and professional doctorate students.

countries: Study in New Zealand (University of Otago).
institutions: Applies across University of Otago departments: Science, Commerce, Health Sciences, Humanities, etc. Example programs: PhD in Biological Sciences, PhD in Law, PhD in Medicine, PhD in Commerce, PhD in Education, PhD in Computer Science.
requirements:
Verbatim from Otago page (excerpt):
"Information: Applicants must usually be seeking their first doctoral qualification; selection based on academic merit and applicant's research potential. Further application process details available on Otago scholarships pages." Full regulations PDF linked on page.

University of Otago Doctoral Scholarship - Application checklist (verbatim excerpts from Otago official pages):

From University of Otago 'How to apply' and 'Supporting documents':
• Completed online application form via Otago portal.
• Academic transcripts for all tertiary study (official electronic copies acceptable initially; final certified copies required upon offer).
• Degree certificates (official certified copies where required).

applicationCycle: Applicants can apply anytime; see Otago scholarship page for details.

title: Singapore International Graduate Award (SINGA)
overview: A*STAR and partner Singapore universities' joint PhD scholarship for international students to pursue research-based doctoral degrees in Singapore.
fundingDetails: Publicized components: tuition covered; monthly stipend commonly ranges ~S$2,200–S$2,700 (varies by host); settling-in allowance and airfare may be provided (amounts vary). Exact values vary by host university—see A*STAR/SINGA pages for details.
countries: Singapore (host country).
institutions: A*STAR research institutes; National University of Singapore (NUS); Nanyang Technological University (NTU); Singapore Management University (SMU); Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD); Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT). Official SINGA page: https://www.a-star.edu.sg/Scholarships/for-graduate-studies/singapore-international-graduate-award-singa
requirements:
From A*STAR/SINGA guidance (excerpt):
"Applicants must submit: online application; CV; academic transcripts; degree certificate; research proposal (if required); names of referees; English proficiency evidence if needed. Shortlisted candidates invited for interview." See host pages for full checklist.

SINGA (A*STAR) - Application checklist (verbatim excerpts from SINGA / A*STAR official pages):

Typical required documents:
• Online application form via SINGA portal.
• Academic transcripts and degree certificates (scanned copies; official originals required upon offer).

applicationCycle: Application windows published on A*STAR and host university SINGA pages; varies by intake.

countries: Taiwan (host country).
institutions: Examples: National Taiwan University; National Cheng Kung University; National Tsing Hua University; National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University; National Taiwan Normal University; National Chengchi University. Official lists in MOE guidelines.

title: Italian Government Scholarships (Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation)
overview: Italian Government (MAECI) scholarships support foreign students for degree and specialization courses in Italy (tuition contribution, living allowance); administered via MAECI and Italian embassies.
fundingDetails: MAECI scholarships typically provide a monthly allowance (amount varies by year; recent figures often around €900–€1,000/month) and tuition contribution — exact current amounts must be checked on MAECI official call PDF. requirements:
From MAECI scholarship call (typical checklist excerpt):
"Application form; curriculum vitae; motivation letter; copy of passport; academic transcripts; proof of language proficiency; other documents as specified in the call." See the annual MAECI call PDF for verbatim checklist and deadlines.

notes: Number of scholarships and value per year specified in MAECI call; recipients may be required to enroll at selected Italian universities and meet visa/health insurance requirements.

fundingDetails: IsDB scholarship components typically include tuition fees, living allowance, monthly stipend, medical insurance, and return ticket — exact amounts may vary per program and host university (not always published centrally).

requirements:

"Applicants must submit: application form; academic transcripts; degree certificates; medical report; recommendation letters; passport copy; other documents specified by the scheme. See IsDB scholarship pages for full checklist and country office requirements."

title: New Zealand International Doctoral Research Scholarships / University doctoral packages (NZ)

fundingDetails: Program covers tuition, room & board, required course fees, travel to/from program and in-country travel; Schwarzman does not publish a single monthly stipend figure centrally (components listed on official site).

institutions: Hosted at Tsinghua University — program: Schwarzman Scholars Master’s in Global Affairs (Tsinghua). Alumni frequently come from institutions like Harvard, Oxford, Stanford etc., but Schwarzman is independent. Official: https://www.schwarzmanscholars.org/
requirements:

Schwarzman Scholars - Application Instructions (verbatim excerpts from Schwarzman 'Application Instructions'):

• Academic transcripts and degree certificates (official copies).
• Passport copy and proof of citizenship.
• One-page research proposal (where required), statement of purpose.
• Proof of employment / income documents (family income certificates) where required.
• Medical certificate (successful applicants will be asked to submit a health certificate).
• Letters of recommendation as required by the host university (number varies; many require two).

Source: https://www.adb.org/work-with-us/careers/japan-scholarship-program/procedures-applying ; example university guideline: https://en.ritsumei.ac.jp/admissions-e/file/ADB.ApplicationGuidelines.pdf
applicationCycle: Application windows and deadlines vary per participating university and are posted on ADB-JSP and university JSP pages.
notes: Scholarship primarily for ADB borrowing member country nationals; scholars required to return to home country after study; conditions vary by university.

title: Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) Scholarship for International Students (Germany)
overview: FES supports students and doctoral candidates (particularly those with socio-political engagement) in Germany via scholarships, mentoring, and networking; non-German students studying in Germany may apply through regional FES offices.
fundingDetails: Benefits and monthly stipend amounts vary by country office; FES Germany pages advise funding includes monthly scholarship payments and support for study-related costs but do not publish a single central global stipend figure. See regional office pages for exact amounts.
countries: Host country: Germany. Open to German and international students studying in Germany (eligibility depends on regional FES office).
institutions: Example host universities where FES scholars study: Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin; Freie Universität Berlin; Universität zu Köln; Universität Bonn; Universität Leipzig; Goethe-Universität Frankfurt. FES scholarship info and contact: https://www.fes.de/studienfoerderung/information-in-english
requirements: Representative excerpt (FES): "Who is eligible? · You are already studying in Germany at the time of your application. · Bachelor's students must submit their first graded performance records. German language skills are absolutely necessary and must be proven." Full details: https://www.fes.de/studienfoerderung/information-in-english
applicationCycle: Constituency/office-specific — consult relevant FES regional office for opening & closing dates: https://www.fes.de/studienfoerderung/information-in-english
notes: Prioritises socio-political engagement; duration and renewal depend on study level and office; recipients must meet German residency/study requirements. See regional pages.

title: KAAD (Katholischer Akademischer Ausländer-Dienst) Scholarships (Germany)
overview: KAAD provides scholarships and support to students and academics from developing countries for Master's, PhD and research stays in Germany, emphasising community leadership and Christian social engagement.
fundingDetails: KAAD offers monthly scholarships, travel allowances and seminar funding; amounts vary by country and scholarship type and are listed on country-specific KAAD pages rather than as a single global amount.
countries: Host country: Germany. Applicants are typically nationals of countries in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Middle East per KAAD target regions.
institutions: KAAD scholars study at German universities including Universität Bonn; Universität zu Köln; Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin; Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München; Technische Universität München; Albert-Ludwigs-Universität Freiburg. KAAD info: https://www.kaad.de/en/
requirements: Representative excerpt (KAAD): "career objectives (max. 300 Character) · Applying on the recommendation of the following institution/person · specific information about religious and social activities · commitment of religious and social activities" Full guidance: https://www.kaad.de/en/scholarship-application
applicationCycle: Deadlines vary by country office and scholarship type. See KAAD country pages and application guidance: https://www.kaad.de/en/stipendien
notes: KAAD often requires church recommendation for certain awards; duration and renewal differ by programme; subject to academic progress and KAAD conditions.

title: Rotary Foundation — Global Grant Scholarships (Rotary)
overview: Rotary supports postgraduate scholarships via Global Grants administered by local Rotary clubs/districts, funding graduate study and research that align with Rotary’s areas of focus and requiring club/district sponsorship.
fundingDetails: Typical Global Grant Scholarship budgets for graduate study approximate US$30,000–US$30,500 per scholar (exact approved grant amount is specified per grant application). See Rotary Grant Centre guidance for details.
countries: Grants can fund study at eligible host institutions worldwide where Rotary clubs/districts collaborate.
institutions: Example host institutions previously funded include University of Cape Town; University of British Columbia; University of Oxford; Makerere University; University of Melbourne; Johns Hopkins University. Rotary scholarships page: https://www.rotary.org/en/our-programs/scholarships
requirements: Representative excerpt (Rotary Grant guidance): "Rotary clubs can apply to The Rotary Foundation for district and global grants to support scholarships." Full grant application instructions: https://my.rotary.org/en/document/rotary-foundation-grants-reference-manual
applicationCycle: Cycles and deadlines are managed by sponsoring district/clubs; contact local Rotary club or district for deadlines. Rotary global grants info: https://www.rotary.org/en/our-programs/scholarships
notes: Global Grants require club/district sponsorship, alignment with areas of focus, and include monitoring/reporting requirements; number of awards depends on district funding.

title: Nanyang President’s Graduate Scholarship (NPGS) / NTU Research Scholarships (Nanyang Technological University, Singapore)
overview: NPGS is NTU’s premier PhD scholarship for outstanding candidates, offering full tuition coverage, a competitive stipend and research support; NTU also offers a variety of research scholarships for masters and PhD students.
fundingDetails: NTU indicates NPGS covers full tuition and provides a monthly stipend and allowances; exact stipend rates are published on NTU pages or in offer letters (typical PhD stipends historically ~SGD 3,200/month but check NTU page for current rates).
countries: Host country: Singapore (NTU). Open to international applicants.
institutions: NPGS supports PhD candidates across NTU colleges: College of Engineering, College of Science, College of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences, College of Business, Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, College of Professional and Continuing Education. NTU NPGS: https://www.ntu.edu.sg/admissions/graduate/financialmatters/scholarships/npgs
requirements: Representative excerpt (NTU NPGS): "The Nanyang President's Graduate Scholarship (NPGS) is a competitive and prestigious scholarship scheme designed to encourage outstanding graduates or final-year students to take their first step towards a leading research career by studying for a full time PhD at NTU." Full guidance: https://www.ntu.edu.sg/admissions/graduate/financialmatters/scholarships/npgs
applicationCycle: NTU PhD admissions and scholarship deadlines published on NTU admissions pages per intake; see NPGS page for details.
notes: Recipients must meet academic progress requirements; scholarships cover duration of the degree and may include conference allowances; details in NTU scholarship pages and offer letters.

title: École Normale Supérieure (ENS) — International Selection Scholarships (France)
overview: ENS runs an International Selection competitive exam to admit outstanding international students to its Master's programmes; selected students receive scholarships, mentoring and stipends depending on ENS site and scheme.
fundingDetails: ENS Lyon 'Ampère' scholarships: €1,000/month for one academic year (example); amounts vary by site and scholarship type—see ENS site pages for exact figures.
countries: Host country: France. Open to international applicants worldwide via ENS international selection exams.
institutions: ENS (Paris, Lyon) programmes include Mathematics, Physics, Biology, Computer Science, Philosophy, Economics. ENS international selection: https://www.ens.psl.eu/en/une-formation-d-exception/admissions/international/international-selection
requirements: Representative excerpt (ENS): "International selection Arts & Humanities: Candidates in Humanities must read and write French easily enough to be able to understand courses and tests..." Full admissions & FAQ: https://www.ens.psl.eu/en/une-formation-d-exception/admissions/international/international-selection/faq-about-international
applicationCycle: Deadlines published annually on ENS international selection page; check the official page for current dates.
notes: Scholarship terms vary by site and scheme; renewable conditions dependent on academic performance. See ENS pages for full terms.

title: Ton Duc Thang University (TDTU) — International Graduate Scholarships (Vietnam)
overview: TDTU offers scholarships to international graduate students, including Full Scholarships (100% tuition + accommodation) and Partial Scholarships (percentage-based tuition/accommodation support) based on merit.
fundingDetails: TDTU Full Scholarship: 100% tuition + full accommodation; Partial Scholarship: 50% tuition + 50% accommodation. Expressed as coverage percentages rather than fixed cash amounts on TDTU pages.
countries: Host country: Vietnam. Open to international applicants globally.
institutions: TDTU graduate programmes include Environmental Engineering, Computer Science, Biotechnology, Civil Engineering, Business Administration, Electrical Engineering. Scholarship info: https://grad.tdtu.edu.vn/en/scholarship/
requirements: Representative excerpt (TDTU): "Full scholarship: support 100% full tuition fee + full accommodation fee." Full details: https://grad.tdtu.edu.vn/en/scholarship/
applicationCycle: Deadlines follow TDTU admissions intake cycles; see graduate admissions and scholarship pages for current dates: https://admission.tdtu.edu.vn/en/graduate/information/apply-for-scholarships
notes: Merit-based, renewable based on performance; duration depends on programme regulations. See TDTU scholarship pages.

title: EPFL (École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne) — Master Excellence Fellowships
overview: EPFL Master Excellence Fellowships recognise outstanding master's applicants and provide significant financial support (fellowships) to pursue EPFL master’s programmes.
fundingDetails: EPFL Master Excellence Fellowship: CHF 10,000 per semester (maximum coverage up to 4 semesters), as published on EPFL pages.
countries: Host country: Switzerland (EPFL). Open to international applicants.
institutions: EPFL master's programmes: MSc Computer Science; MSc Data Science; MSc Mechanical Engineering; MSc Electrical Engineering; MSc Life Sciences and Technology; MSc Environmental Engineering. EPFL page: https://www.epfl.ch/education/master/master-excellence-fellowships/
requirements: Representative excerpt (EPFL): "Amount: CHF 10,000 per semester, with a maximum coverage of 4 semesters." Full 'How to apply' page: https://www.epfl.ch/education/master/master-excellence-fellowships/how-to-apply/
applicationCycle: EPFL master's application windows and Fellowship deadlines published annually on EPFL 'How to apply' page.
notes: Fellowships cover up to 4 semesters; recipients must meet academic standards; other benefits such as housing reservation for external candidates may apply.

title: Institut Polytechnique de Paris (IP-Paris) — Doctoral Package / Full Package
overview: IP-Paris offers structured doctoral funding packages (Doctoral Package / Full Package) combining Master's excellence scholarships and a three-year doctoral funding commitment to support integrated MSc+PhD trajectories.
fundingDetails: Doctoral Package: total minimum gross funding approximately €82,800 over 3 years for the doctoral period; Master's excellence amounts additional per package—see IP-Paris pages for exact breakdown.
countries: Host country: France. Open to international applicants admitted to IP-Paris programmes.
institutions: Constituent schools: École Polytechnique; ENSTA Paris; Télécom Paris; Télécom SudParis; ENSAE Paris; Institut Mines-Télécom. IP-Paris pages: https://www.ip-paris.fr/en/education/scholarships
requirements: Representative excerpt (IP-Paris): "Doctoral Package (DP): 3-year doctoral funding commitment, covering only the doctoral studies (a total minimum gross funding of approximately €82,800 over 3 years)." Full page: https://www.ip-paris.fr/en/education/scholarships
applicationCycle: IP-Paris scholarship calls and deadlines published on IP-Paris scholarship pages per intake year.
notes: Doctoral package conditions include enrolment, academic progress and contract terms; see IP-Paris pages for full terms.

title: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI) — Full Scholarships
overview: MBZUAI offers fully-funded Master's and PhD scholarships to admitted students, including tuition waivers, stipends, housing support and research funding, funded by the UAE government.
fundingDetails: MBZUAI states scholarships cover tuition, stipend and related benefits; specific stipend amounts are provided in admission/scholarship guidelines or offer letters rather than a single public global figure.
countries: Host country: United Arab Emirates (Abu Dhabi). Open to international applicants worldwide.
institutions: MBZUAI programmes: M.Sc. in Artificial Intelligence; M.Sc. in Computer Vision; M.Sc. in NLP; PhD in AI; Research tracks in Robotics and Computational Biology. Admissions & scholarships: https://mbzuai.ac.ae/study/graduate-admission-process/
requirements: Representative excerpt (MBZUAI): "MBZUAI is fully funded by the UAE Government and all admitted full-time students are eligible for a range of scholarships." Full details: https://mbzuai.ac.ae/study/graduate-admission-process/
applicationCycle: MBZUAI publishes admissions cycles and priority scholarship deadlines on its admissions pages; consult site for current dates.
notes: Scholarship renewal and benefits depend on academic progress and offer terms; housing and visa assistance commonly provided.

title: Chulalongkorn University — International Graduate Scholarships (Thailand)
overview: Chulalongkorn University offers several scholarships for international graduate students (ASEAN and non-ASEAN), covering tuition, stipends and fees depending on the scheme and faculty.
fundingDetails: Scholarship specifics vary by faculty; some schemes provide full tuition waivers and monthly stipends as published in faculty-level calls and official PDF announcements.
countries: Host country: Thailand. Open to ASEAN and non-ASEAN international applicants depending on scheme.
institutions: Chulalongkorn faculties: Medicine; Engineering; Arts; Social Sciences; Business Administration; Science. Scholarship listings: https://www.chula.ac.th/en/academics/admissions/scholarships/
requirements: Representative excerpt (Chulalongkorn): "Applicants must be international graduate students from ASEAN or Non-ASEAN countries... Recipients are not allowed to accept any off-campus employment in Thailand during the term of the scholarship." Full faculty PDF: https://www.ahs.chula.ac.th/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/scholarship-grad-09-20231220040211_198.pdf
applicationCycle: Deadlines vary by faculty and scholarship scheme; consult Chulalongkorn pages for exact dates.
notes: Terms depend on specific scholarship; recipients must follow university rules and visa conditions.

title: Beijing Government Scholarship (BGS) for international students in Beijing universities (China)
overview: The Beijing Government Scholarship funds international students studying at Beijing institutions offering tuition and living-cost support across categories, administered by Beijing Municipal Education Commission and host universities.
fundingDetails: BGS categories include top awards up to RMB 40,000/year for certain levels and RMB 20,000/year for others; see Beijing government and host university pages for exact category amounts.
countries: Host country: China (Beijing). Open to international applicants worldwide applying to participating Beijing universities.
institutions: Participating universities include Peking University; Tsinghua University; Beijing Normal University; Beihang University; Beijing University of Chemical Technology; Beijing Institute of Technology. Info: https://english.beijing.gov.cn/studyinginbeijing/scholarshipapplications/202006/t20200617_1926391.html
requirements: Representative excerpt (Beijing gov): "The applicants must fill in and submit the following documents truly, correctly and completely:" Full instructions: https://english.beijing.gov.cn/studyinginbeijing/scholarshipapplications/202006/t20200617_1926391.html
applicationCycle: Deadlines differ by host university; many accept BGS documents by end-February for typical intakes—see Beijing government and university pages for exact dates.
notes: Eligibility includes age limits by degree (e.g., MSc under 35, PhD under 40), academic requirements and other conditions; see official pages for full terms.

title: Chancellor’s International Scholarships (University of Warwick, UK)
overview: Warwick's Chancellor's International Scholarships support outstanding full-time doctoral students by covering fees and providing a maintenance stipend plus a Research, Training and Support Grant.
fundingDetails: CIS typically covers academic fees and maintenance stipend at prevailing UKRI rates for up to 3.5 years and includes a one-off RTSG of £5,000 as published on Warwick pages.
countries: Host country: United Kingdom. Open to overseas fee-status PhD applicants at University of Warwick.
institutions: Departments include Warwick Business School; Computer Science; School of Law; Economics; Life Sciences; History. Warwick CIS info: https://warwick.ac.uk/services/dc/schols_fund/new/cistc/
requirements: Representative excerpt (Warwick CIS): "The CIS is normally awarded for up to 3.5 years of full-time doctoral study and normally includes the payment of both academic fees and a maintenance grant to the award-holder. A one-off Research, Training and Supporting Grant (RTSG) of £5,000 to fund research-related expenses." Full terms: https://warwick.ac.uk/services/dc/schols_fund/new/cistc/
applicationCycle: Scholarship competitions align with doctoral admissions cycles; see Warwick Doctoral College competitions page for current dates: https://warwick.ac.uk/services/dc/schols_fund/applicants/competitions/
notes: Awards are conditional on academic progress; full details in scholarship offer letters and Doctoral College terms.

title: FCT — Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia PhD Studentships (Portugal)
overview: FCT funds PhD studentships in Portugal through competitive calls (Regular Line and Specific Lines), financing doctoral research training and placements in academic and non-academic settings.
fundingDetails: FCT studentship stipend amounts and contract terms are published in each call notice (monthly stipend and contract type). See 2025/2026 call pages for exact figures.
countries: Host country: Portugal. Open to eligible Portuguese and foreign applicants per call eligibility.
institutions: Host institutions: Universidade de Lisboa; Universidade do Porto; NOVA University Lisbon; University of Coimbra; University of Aveiro; University of Minho. FCT call page: https://www.fct.pt/en/concursos/concurso-bolsas-de-doutoramento-2025-linha-de-candidatura-geral
requirements: Representative excerpt (FCT call): "To apply for a PhD studentship, it is mandatory: Not to have benefited from any PhD studentship or PhD in Industry studentship directly funded by FCT, Not to hold a doctoral degree, To live in Portugal permanently or habitually, if applying to both in Portugal and abroad or entirely abroad studentship." Full call: https://www.fct.pt/en/concursos/concurso-bolsas-de-doutoramento-2025-linha-de-candidatura-geral
applicationCycle: Example 2025 Regular Line call: March 17 to April 16, 2025. See FCT call pages for current dates.
notes: Duration typically 3-4 years; eligibility and reporting obligations specified in the call notice and regulations.

title: TU Delft — Justus & Louise van Effen Excellence Scholarships (Delft University of Technology, Netherlands)
overview: The Justus & Louise van Effen Excellence Scholarships support excellent international applicants to selected TU Delft MSc programmes with tuition and living-cost contributions.
fundingDetails: TU Delft scholarship coverage varies by call; awards typically include tuition fee waiver and living-cost contribution; exact amounts published per call on TU Delft scholarship pages.
countries: Host country: Netherlands. Eligible: excellent international applicants to TU Delft MSc programmes (non-EU/EEA applicants typically).
institutions: Example MSc programmes: MSc Civil Engineering; MSc Mechanical Engineering; MSc Computer Science; MSc Applied Earth Sciences; MSc Architectural Engineering; MSc Chemical Engineering. TU Delft scholarships: https://www.tudelft.nl/en/education/practical-matters/scholarships/
requirements: Representative excerpt (TU Delft): "Applicants must be excellent international applicants (conditionally) admitted to one of the 2-year regular TU Delft MSc programmes." Full details: https://www.tudelft.nl/en/education/practical-matters/scholarships/
applicationCycle: Application and award deadlines are published on TU Delft scholarship pages and per faculty for each intake year.
notes: Eligibility and renewal conditions depend on academic performance and programme regulations; exact terms on TU Delft pages.

title: EPFL — PhD funding (doctoral assistant posts / PhD salaries)
overview: EPFL funds PhD students through doctoral assistant contracts (employed positions) or external fellowships; doctoral assistants receive salaries and social benefits as EPFL employees.
fundingDetails: EPFL salary scale (effective 01/01/2026): starting gross annual salary CHF 55,225 for doctoral assistants (including 13th month); increments and scales on EPFL HR pages.
countries: Host country: Switzerland. Open to international PhD applicants at EPFL.
institutions: PhD positions across EPFL laboratories in Physics, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Materials Science. EPFL PhD & salary pages: https://www.epfl.ch/education/phd/doctoral-studies-structure/doctoral-students-salary/
requirements: Representative excerpt (EPFL salaries): "The basic starting salary of an EPFL doctoral assistant is CHF 55'225. - gross per annum (valid from 01/01/2026)." Full page: https://www.epfl.ch/education/phd/doctoral-studies-structure/doctoral-students-salary/
applicationCycle: PhD positions advertised year-round by labs and departments; application deadlines vary per position.
notes: Doctoral assistants are employed for fixed-term contracts (typically 3-4 years), with social security benefits and renewal conditions per contract. See EPFL HR pages.

title: PTDF — Petroleum Technology Development Fund (Nigeria) — Overseas Postgraduate Scholarship (MSc & PhD)
overview: PTDF awards Overseas Postgraduate Scholarships to Nigerian candidates for MSc and PhD studies in oil & gas-related disciplines at PTDF-approved partner institutions abroad, aiming to build technical capacity for Nigeria's petroleum sector.
fundingDetails: PTDF scholarship benefits include tuition fees, living allowance, book allowance, return flights and health insurance; exact allowance amounts and rates are specified in PTDF guidelines and call documents on the PTDF portal.
countries: Approved host countries include UK, Germany, France, Malaysia and other approved destinations as per PTDF partner list; see PTDF portal for full list.
institutions: PTDF-approved institutions include Imperial College London; University of Aberdeen; Technical University Munich; RWTH Aachen; TU Delft; Université de Lorraine. PTDF portal: https://scholarship.ptdf.gov.ng/
requirements: Representative excerpt (PTDF guidelines): "Applications are limited to PTDF-approved courses at PTDF partner institutions. Scholarships will only be awarded for studies relevant to PTDF’s mandate; candidates may be disqualified for programmes deemed not relevant to the oil and gas industry." Full guidelines: https://scholarship.ptdf.gov.ng/guidelines
applicationCycle: PTDF publishes annual calls; example 2026 Overseas Scholarship call announced in Jan 2026. See PTDF scholarship portal for current dates: https://scholarship.ptdf.gov.ng/
notes: PTDF requires scholars to return to Nigeria and serve in relevant sectors post-study; duration and obligations in PTDF guidelines.

title: Commonwealth PhD Scholarships (Commonwealth Scholarship Commission, UK)
overview: Commonwealth PhD Scholarships support doctoral research for candidates from eligible Commonwealth countries, prioritising development impact and providing tuition, living allowance and return travel for scholars at UK institutions.
fundingDetails: CSC PhD awards cover tuition fees, a living allowance (stipend at CSC rates), approved airfare and other allowances; exact stipend and dependent allowances are detailed in CSC guidance documents.
countries: Host country: United Kingdom. Eligible applicants are citizens of Commonwealth countries (specific eligibility categories apply per call).
institutions: CSC scholars study at universities across the UK: University of Oxford; University of Cambridge; University of Manchester; King's College London; University of Edinburgh; University of Nottingham. CSC PhD page: https://cscuk.fcdo.gov.uk/scholarships/commonwealth-phd-scholarships-for-least-developed-countries-and-vulnerable-states/
requirements: Representative excerpt (CSC): "Applications to the CSC are made using the application system, CSC Central. The CSC is unable to accept any applications or documentation not submitted via CSC Central." Full guidance: https://cscuk.fcdo.gov.uk/apply/
applicationCycle: CSC application windows published annually; example 2026/27 cycle opened early September and closed mid-October 2025. See CSC pages for current cycle dates.
notes: CSC terms include typical duration up to 3.5 years, development focus, reporting obligations and possible dependent allowances; full details in CSC guidance.

title: Japan–Africa Dream Scholarship (JADS) — AfDB + Japanese partners
overview: A jointly run AfDB and Japanese partners scholarship providing fully-funded Master-level opportunities to African nationals to study in Japan in development-related fields. It emphasises leadership, professional experience and contributions to Africa's development.
fundingDetails: Official AfDB pages list covered benefits (tuition, economy-class return airfare, medical insurance, monthly stipend) but do NOT publish fixed numeric stipend amounts on the AfDB JADS pages. See AfDB benefits page for itemised coverage.
countries: Host country: Japan; applicants must be nationals of AfDB member (African) countries.
institutions: Examples: University of Tokyo; Kyoto University; Osaka University; Tohoku University; University of Tsukuba; Waseda University. Authoritative list: https://www.afdb.org/en/about-us/careers/japan-africa-dream-scholarship-jads-program/list-of-universities
requirements: Representative excerpt (AfDB JADS): "Begin gathering required documentation for the scholarship application, including: your current curriculum vitae, proof of employment for each record of employment, certified copies of academic transcripts and degree certificates, a signed letter of application, a vision statement, three letters of recommendation..." Full: https://www.afdb.org/en/about-us/careers/japan-africa-dream-scholarship-jads-program/application-procedures
applicationCycle: Central application: See AfDB JADS announcements and calls (dates vary by intake): https://www.afdb.org/en/about-us/careers/japan-africa-dream-scholarship-jads-program
notes: Eligibility includes 1+ year paid employment after Bachelor's, age limits may apply per call; scholarship covers duration of degree and includes medical insurance; see AfDB JADS 'Who is eligible' and 'Scholarship benefits'. https://www.afdb.org/en/about-us/careers/japan-africa-dream-scholarship-jads-program

title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
overview: A global initiative implemented through partner universities and NGOs to support academically talented, service-oriented young people (primarily from Africa) with scholarships and wrap-around support for undergraduate and postgraduate study.
fundingDetails: Funding varies by partner. Mastercard Foundation lists covered benefits (tuition, accommodation, mentoring, travel) but no single global stipend figure is published centrally.
countries: Available via partner universities across Africa, North America, Europe and elsewhere—apply through partner institutions.
