institutions: Examples: Arizona State University; Ashesi University; Carnegie Mellon University Africa; McGill University; University of Cape Town; University of British Columbia. Partners list: https://mastercardfdn.org/en/what-we-do/our-programs/mastercard-foundation-scholars-program/where-to-apply/
requirements: Representative excerpt (Mastercard Foundation): "The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program is implemented through our partner universities and non-governmental organizations. The application process and decision-making are managed individually by each partner. If you are interested in becoming a Mastercard Foundation Scholar, please apply directly through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program-affiliated institution you wish to attend." Full partner guidance: https://mastercardfdn.org/en/what-we-do/our-programs/mastercard-foundation-scholars-program/where-to-apply/
applicationCycle: Constituency/partner-specific — see partner pages: https://mastercardfdn.org/en/what-we-do/our-programs/mastercard-foundation-scholars-program/where-to-apply/
notes: Program delivered by partners; coverage and terms are partner-specific (tuition, accommodation, mentoring, etc.). See partner listings for details.

title: Pan-African University (PAU) / African Union Scholarships
overview: Pan-African University (PAU), an African Union initiative, offers fully-funded postgraduate scholarships across continental institutes to develop high-level skills and research capacity for Africa's development.
fundingDetails: PAU states scholarships cover tuition, living expenses and medical insurance; call documents include stipend details but a single global numeric stipend is not published centrally on AU/PAU landing pages.
countries: Open to nationals of African Union member states; host institutes across Africa.
institutions: Examples of PAU institutes: Ibadan (Nigeria), Nairobi (Kenya), Algeria, Yaoundé (Cameroon), Accra (Ghana), Cairo (Egypt). Authoritative listing and submission portal: https://pau-au.africa/ and https://www.au-pau.org/submission/
requirements: Representative excerpt (PAU Call): "Certified copies of relevant certificates and transcripts, Passport or National I.D. card (biometric/personal details page), Clear coloured passport size photograph (2cmx2cm), Detailed CV, Recommendation letters from 2 Professors." Full call: https://pau-au.africa/news-events/news/news-detail?cHash=1f91429bbbecb69cf54c6a8c35ba1df3&tx_news_pi1%5Bnews%5D=578
applicationCycle: PAU Call example (2026‑27): launched 15 Nov 2025 — closed 15 Dec 2025. See current call page and submission portal: https://pau-au.africa/news-events/news/news-detail?cHash=1f91429bbbecb69cf54c6a8c35ba1df3&tx_news_pi1%5Bnews%5D=578 and https://www.au-pau.org/submission/
notes: PAU scholarships cover medical insurance; selection criteria, age limits and number of awards are in the Call document. Duration equals programme length. See call for full terms.

title: Aga Khan Foundation — International Scholarship Programme (Master's)
overview: The Aga Khan Foundation's International Scholarship Programme (ISP) provides limited, competitive scholarships (commonly 50% grant / 50% loan) to outstanding students from selected developing countries for postgraduate studies at reputable institutions worldwide.
fundingDetails: AKF states awards are commonly disbursed as 50% grant and 50% loan; exact amounts are determined case-by-case and not published as fixed global figures on AKF ISP pages.
countries: Open to applicants from selected developing countries (country lists and office contacts available on AKF pages).
institutions: AKF does not publish a single participating institutions list. Example institutions attended by alumni: University of Oxford; University of Toronto; University of British Columbia; LSE; University of Melbourne; National University of Singapore. Official AKF ISP page: https://akf.org/international-scholarship-programme/
requirements: Representative excerpt (AKF ISP): "The application procedures of AKF's International Scholarship Programme are decentralised. Students may obtain application forms as of 1st January each year from AKF offices or Aga Khan Education Services / Boards in their countries of current residence. Completed applications should be returned to the agency from which the form was obtained. They should not be sent to Geneva." Full guidance: https://akf.org/international-scholarship-programme/
applicationCycle: Decentralised — country office deadlines vary. Forms available from 1 January each year per AKF page: https://akf.org/international-scholarship-programme/
notes: Scholarships awarded based on merit and need; often 50% loan repayable; duration equals programme length; consult local AKF office for specific terms.

title: Ignacy Łukasiewicz Scholarship Programme (Poland / NAWA)
overview: A Polish government scholarship (NAWA) for selected students from developing countries to undertake tuition-free Master's (second-cycle) studies in Poland, often including a preparatory Polish language course.
fundingDetails: NAWA indicates coverage of tuition and preparatory course
countries: Host country: Poland. Eligible nationalities: list published on NAWA's programme page.
institutions: Examples: University of Warsaw; Jagiellonian University; Warsaw University of Technology; Wrocław University of Science and Technology; Adam Mickiewicz University; AGH University of Science and Technology. NAWA programme page: https://nawa.gov.pl/en/students/foreign-students/the-ignacy-lukasiewicz-scholarship-programme
requirements: Representative excerpt (NAWA): "Application process for the Ignacy Łukasiewicz Scholarship Programme is operated by NAWA’s ICT system, available at https://programs.nawa.gov.pl/login." Full checklist in call documents: https://nawa.gov.pl/en/students/foreign-students/the-ignacy-lukasiewicz-scholarship-programme
applicationCycle: Annual calls; apply via NAWA ICT system. See NAWA programme page for current dates.
notes: Includes preparatory Polish language course; selection per call; duration equals programme length; full terms in NAWA downloadable documents.

title: Czech Government Scholarships (Ministry of Foreign Affairs / Ministry of Education)
overview: The Government of the Czech Republic offers development scholarships to citizens of selected developing countries to pursue bachelor’s, follow-up master’s or doctoral studies at Czech public universities under its Foreign Development Cooperation programme.
fundingDetails: Scholarship covers full-time study length and includes a preparatory Czech language course when required; exact stipend amounts are published in 'Guidelines for Granting Scholarships' PDFs on the ministry site.
countries: Eligible countries are listed on the ministry page (see MŠMT programme page).
institutions: Examples: Charles University, Masaryk University, Czech Technical University in Prague, Palacký University Olomouc, University of Ostrava, University of Economics Prague. Official page: https://msmt.gov.cz/eu-and-international-affairs/government-scholarships-developing-countries
requirements: Representative excerpt (MŠMT): "Each applicant is obliged to fill in an electronic application form by 30 September [year]. The application is considered complete only after completing the general knowledge test and, if applicable, the English language test." Full Guidelines PDF: https://msmt.gov.cz/eu-and-international-affairs/government-scholarships-developing-countries
applicationCycle: Example: Deadline for 2026/2027 applications: 30 September 2025. See ministry page for current cycles: https://msmt.gov.cz/eu-and-international-affairs/government-scholarships-developing-countries
notes: Guidelines include programme length coverage, entrance requirements and more in downloadable PDFs on the ministry page.

title: Leiden University — Leiden Excellence Scholarship (LExS)
overview: LExS is a competitive Leiden University award for outstanding non-EEA students enrolling in full-time master's degree programmes, providing partial scholarships to reduce tuition or provide contributions toward study costs.
fundingDetails: Leiden states LExS award levels vary by faculty; no single fixed global stipend is published centrally—award amounts are listed per faculty via application screens.
countries: Host country: Netherlands. Eligible: non-EEA/non-EFTA applicants to eligible Leiden master's programmes.
institutions: Example eligible masters: LLM International & European Law; MSc International Development Studies; MSc Computer Science; MA International Relations; MSc Environmental Science; MA Archaeology. Official page: https://www.universiteitleiden.nl/en/scholarships/sea/leiden-university-excellence-scholarship-lexs
requirements: Representative excerpt (Leiden LExS): "1. Apply online for admission to a Master's programme at Leiden University and pay the application fee if applicable. 2. After submitting your application for admission, return to the Apply screen in your online application and click on Scholarships. If you are eligible to apply for the LExS, it will now be visible in the scholarships section." Full page: https://www.universiteitleiden.nl/en/scholarships/sea/leiden-university-excellence-scholarship-lexs
applicationCycle: Depends on faculty/programme deadlines. Apply for admission by programme deadlines and apply for LExS via online application portal.
notes: LExS limited in number and generally not full funding; recipients must show proof of sufficient funds for visa/residence permit. Duration equals programme length; renewal conditions per faculty.

title: Utrecht University — Utrecht Excellence Scholarships (Netherlands)
overview: The Utrecht Excellence Scholarship (UES) is a selective Utrecht University award for top international students (non-EEA) to pursue master’s degrees; it may cover tuition or tuition plus the income required for residence permit in the Netherlands.
fundingDetails: UES awards either (a) tuition fee, OR (b) tuition fee plus the income required for a one-year period as set by the Dutch IND. Exact amounts are programme-dependent and tied to IND rates.
countries: Host country: Netherlands. Eligible: non-EEA students applying to eligible Utrecht master's programmes.
institutions: Example masters: MSc Sustainable Development; MSc Governance; MSc Information Studies; MSc Molecular and Cellular Life Sciences; MSc Earth and Sustainability; MSc Economics of Sustainability. Official page: https://www.uu.nl/en/masters/general-information/application-and-admission/scholarships-and-grants/utrecht-excellence-scholarships
requirements: Representative excerpt (Utrecht UES): "To be eligible for a Utrecht Excellence Scholarship, you must belong to the top 10% of your graduating class; you must not hold an EU/EEA passport..." Full details: https://www.uu.nl/en/masters/general-information/application-and-admission/scholarships-and-grants/utrecht-excellence-scholarships
applicationCycle: Programme-specific deadlines; UES may be suspended for some intakes—see Utrecht page for current status.
notes: UES may be renewable for second year; amounts tied to tuition and IND rates; see Utrecht page for full policy.

title: Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation scholarships
overview: Gulbenkian Foundation offers multiple scholarships and research grants (including Armenian Studies, Portuguese culture research grants and short-term grants) supporting Master's, PhD and postdoctoral research and short-term research stays.
fundingDetails: Example (official): Calouste Gulbenkian Armenian Studies Scholarship: fixed annual grant €4,000 (for some categories) and Short-Term Grants: maximum €5,000 (see call documents). See Gulbenkian call PDFs for exact amounts.
countries: Typically in Portugal or host institutions worldwide depending on the call; eligibility varies by call.
institutions: Examples of host/partner institutions that have held Gulbenkian-supported projects: University of Lisbon; NOVA University Lisbon; University of Porto; University of Coimbra; ISCTE-IUL; Fundação Calouste Gulbenkian research partners. Gulbenkian scholarships list: https://gulbenkian.pt/en/bolsas-lista/ and specific programme page: https://gulbenkian.pt/en/bolsas-lista/calouste-gulbenkian-armenian-studies-scholarship/
requirements: Representative excerpt (Gulbenkian Armenian Studies call conditions PDF): "Candidates wishing to apply... must: Be admitted to, or already pursuing, a Master's or PhD degree, or a Postdoctoral project..." Full Call Conditions PDF: https://cdn.gulbenkian.pt/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/conditions-Armenian-studies-scholarship-2025-2.pdf
applicationCycle: Example call dates (Armenian Studies): 02 Mar 2026 – 15 May 2026. See Gulbenkian calls listing for current deadlines: https://gulbenkian.pt/en/bolsas-lista/
notes: Call conditions specify duration (up to 3 years for some awards), number of awards (e.g., up to 50 in some categories), and grant amounts; see call PDFs for full terms. https://gulbenkian.pt/en/bolsas-lista/

title: Australia Awards (AUSAID) / Australia Awards Scholarships
overview: Australia Awards Scholarships (administered by DFAT) are long-term awards for individuals from partner developing countries to undertake full-time undergraduate or postgraduate study at participating Australian institutions, with leadership development and return-service expectations.
fundingDetails: The Australia Awards Policy Handbook details benefits: full tuition fees, establishment allowance, return air travel, health coverage and a living allowance; exact monetary amounts and allowance rates vary by country and are defined in country profiles and the Policy Handbook: https://www.dfat.gov.au/people-to-people/australia-awards/australia-awards-scholarships and Policy Handbook PDF: https://www.dfat.gov.au/sites/default/files/aus-awards-scholarships-policy-handbook.pdf
countries: Host country: Australia. Eligible applicants: citizens/residents of participating countries listed on DFAT 'Participating countries' page: https://www.dfat.gov.au/people-to-people/australia-awards/participating-countries
institutions: Examples of Australian host universities: Australian National University; University of Melbourne; UNSW Sydney; University of Sydney; Monash University; University of Queensland. Further details and country-specific participating institutions are on DFAT and Australia Awards country pages.
requirements: Representative excerpt (DFAT 'How to apply'): "When you register online, you will be required to answer some questions to establish your eligibility. You will then be given a unique registration number, username and password... Check the opening and closing dates for your country..." Full handbook & country profiles: https://www.dfat.gov.au/people-to-people/australia-awards/how-to-apply-for-an-australia-awards-scholarship and https://www.dfat.gov.au/sites/default/files/aus-awards-scholarships-policy-handbook.pdf
applicationCycle: Country-specific opening/closing dates. Example 2027 round: applications open 1 Feb 2026; many country deadlines 30 Apr 2026 — see 'Opening and closing dates' and country profile pages: https://www.dfat.gov.au/people-to-people/australia-awards/australia-awards-scholarships-opening-and-closing-dates and https://www.dfat.gov.au/people-to-people/australia-awards/participating-countries
notes: Contractual obligations include return-service to applicants' home countries and adherence to the Australia Awards Policy Handbook. Scholarships cover program length, with conditions and allowances detailed in the Policy Handbook.

title: Qatar Scholarships / Qatar University & QNRF PhD-related scholarships
overview: Qatar Scholarships (central portal) and university-sponsored scholarships (e.g., Qatar University, HBKU, KU) provide tuition waivers, stipends, accommodation and travel for eligible international and Qatari students; QNRF also funds graduate research awards and supports graduate students through GSRA.
fundingDetails: Qatar Scholarships portal and university pages list benefits: full tuition waiver, round-trip airfare, furnished accommodation, monthly stipend and medical insurance. Example HBKU official stipend table lists monthly amounts (e.g., International PhD 9,000 QAR/month). QNRF GSRA guidelines define registration and funding procedures (see PDF).
countries: Host country: Qatar. Some schemes target residents or nationals; others accept international applicants from developing countries
institutions: Examples of participating Qatar institutions: Qatar University; Hamad Bin Khalifa University (HBKU); Khalifa University (in UAE but regional); Education City partner universities; Lusail University; Doha Institute. Qatar Scholarships programs listing: https://www.qatarscholarships.qa/programs/ and university-specific pages.
requirements: Representative excerpt (qatarscholarships.qa 'Who can apply'): "Students admitted to the Qatar scholarship program are typically be provided with: One round-trip ticket from their home-country to Qatar at the beginning and end of their programs; Furnished accommodation; Full tuition waiver; Local transportation to classes (as required); Monthly stipend." Full details: https://www.qatarscholarships.qa/who-can-apply/ and QNRF GSRA PDF: https://www.udst.edu.qa/sites/default/files/2025-04/GSRA%20Procedure%20-%20QRDI%20Portal.pdf
applicationCycle: Deadlines follow university admissions timelines or Qatar Scholarships portal guidance; contact university or see portal 'Programs' and 'FAQ' pages for timelines: https://www.qatarscholarships.qa/ and university scholarship pages (e.g., https://www.qu.edu.qa/en-us/students/admission/scholarships).
notes: Conditions and continuity rules vary by scheme and institution. HBKU provides detailed stipend tables and conditions in its scholarship guidelines: https://www.hbku.edu.qa/en/scholarship and QNRF outlines GSRA registration and award management in PDF.

title: Khalifa University Graduate Scholarships (UAE) — Master's & PhD
overview: Khalifa University provides merit-based graduate scholarships to Master's and PhD students which typically include tuition coverage, a monthly stipend, accommodation support, medical insurance and travel allowances.
fundingDetails: Khalifa University official scholarship FAQs and pages indicate tuition waivers and competitive monthly stipends; university pages and scholarship FAQs specify that stipends and allowances vary by award tier—see Khalifa University scholarship pages for exact amounts and tiers. (Official KU pages list stipend ranges and scholarship benefits.)
countries: Host country: United Arab Emirates — open to international applicants worldwide.
institutions: Khalifa University degree offerings include: College of Engineering; College of Science; College of Medicine and Health Sciences; Graduate Programs in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Computer Science, Materials Science. Graduate scholarships info: https://www.ku.ac.ae/scholarships-postgraduate and Graduate Catalog: https://ku-ae.smartcatalogiq.com/en/2023-2024/graduate-catalog/graduate-admission-and-scholarships
requirements: Representative excerpt (Khalifa 'Scholarship FAQs'): "Waiver of tuition fees up to 100%. Competitive monthly stipend for high caliber UAE National and international students. Health insurance for full-time..." Full admissions & scholarship policies: https://ku-ae.smartcatalogiq.com/en/2023-2024/graduate-catalog/graduate-admission-and-scholarships and https://www.ku.ac.ae/scholarship-faqs
applicationCycle: Admission and scholarship application deadlines published on Khalifa graduate admissions portals; refer to Postgraduate Admissions and scholarships pages for current deadlines: https://www.ku.ac.ae/postgraduate-admissions and https://www.ku.ac.ae/scholarships-postgraduate
notes: Scholarship benefits may include mobilization/demobilization flights, visa fee coverage, medical insurance, and obligations to meet academic progression criteria; see KU scholarship pages for exact terms.

title: African Union / African Development Bank (General AU & AfDB scholarship initiatives)
overview: The African Union and African Development Bank run continent-wide scholarship initiatives (e.g., Pan-African University scholarships, AfDB scholarship drives, partnership scholarships) to build African capacity through fully-funded postgraduate fellowships.
fundingDetails: AU and AfDB calls list coverage as tuition, living stipend and insurance; specific stipend amounts and number of awards are provided in individual call PDFs (e.g., PAU call document).
countries: Open to nationals of AU member states; host institutions across Africa and partner overseas institutions depending on programme.
institutions: Examples: PAU institutes (Ibadan; Nairobi; Yaoundé; Accra; Algeria; Cairo); AfDB partner universities for JADS and SHOU; see AU and AfDB pages: https://au.int/en/announcements/ and https://www.afdb.org/en/news-and-events
requirements: Representative excerpt (PAU Call): "Certified copies of relevant certificates and transcripts, Passport or National I.D. card (biometric/personal details page), Clear coloured passport size photograph (2cmx2cm), Detailed CV, Recommendation letters from 2 Professors." Full AU/PAU call: https://pau-au.africa/news-events/news/news-detail?cHash=1f91429bbbecb69cf54c6a8c35ba1df3&tx_news_pi1%5Bnews%5D=578
applicationCycle: See AU/PAU and AfDB calls for cycle dates; examples: PAU call 15 Nov 2025 – 15 Dec 2025. AfDB-Shanghai Ocean University call closed 10 Dec 2025. Link central call pages: https://au.int/en/announcements/ and https://www.afdb.org/en/news-and-events/african-development-bank-group-and-shanghai-ocean-university-announce-2026-scholarship-drive-african-talent-89021
notes: Program-specific conditions: many require return-to-continent service commitment; duration equals program length; medical insurance provided. See call PDFs for detailed terms.

title: Weidenfeld‑Hoffmann Scholarships & Leadership Programme (University of Oxford)
overview: A fully-funded Oxford scholarship and leadership programme supporting outstanding graduates from developing countries to pursue master’s degrees at Oxford with leadership training and mentoring.
fundingDetails: Weidenfeld‑Hoffmann scholarships cover full course fees and a grant for living costs (Oxford states a living-costs grant of at least £15,609 – see Oxford Weidenfeld page).
countries: Host country: United Kingdom (University of Oxford). Open to candidates from eligible developing countries worldwide.
institutions: Weidenfeld‑Hoffmann funds students across many Oxford master's programmes. Example departments: Blavatnik School of Government; Department of International Development; Said Business School; Department of Public Policy; Department of Politics and International Relations; School of Geography and the Environment. Official Oxford page: https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/fees-and-funding/fees-funding-and-scholarship-search/weidenfeld-hoffmann-scholarships-and-leadership-programme
requirements: Representative excerpt (Weidenfeld‑Hoffmann / Oxford): "Applications must be submitted to the University of Oxford by the University or course deadline. In addition to applying for admission, candidates must submit the Weidenfeld‑Hoffmann Trust online application form by the Trust deadline." Full guidance: https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/fees-and-funding/fees-funding-and-scholarship-search/weidenfeld-hoffmann-scholarships-and-leadership-programme
applicationCycle: Applicants must meet University of Oxford course deadlines (often Jan) and the Trust application deadline; check Oxford and Weidenfeld‑Hoffmann pages for current cycle dates.
notes: Scholarship covers course fees and living costs; leadership programme includes mentoring; number of awards varies, and college allocation may apply. See Oxford page for full conditions.

title: Okinawa Institute of Science & Technology (OIST) Graduate School Scholarships
overview: OIST Graduate School offers fully-funded PhD positions with comprehensive financial packages (research assistantships covering stipend, tuition waiver, housing support and travel allowances) to international applicants.
fundingDetails: Official OIST Student Support page: "Financial support starts at approximately 2.6 million JPY (taxable) in the first year and will increase with academic progress up to approximately 3.6 million JPY (taxable)." (OIST official page).
countries: Host country: Japan. Open to applicants of all nationalities.
institutions: OIST offers interdisciplinary PhD programmes; example research units include Physics, Neuroscience, Biology, Chemistry, Mathematics, Computer Science. Admissions and student support pages: https://www.oist.jp/admissions and https://www.oist.jp/education/student-support
requirements: Representative excerpt (OIST PhD admissions): "The basic requirement for the OIST Graduate Program is completion of a bachelor's, master's, or equivalent degree before the admission date..." Full admissions and student support details: https://www.oist.jp/admissions/phd-program/apply-phd and https://www.oist.jp/education/student-support
applicationCycle: OIST operates deadline windows for PhD admissions—check 'Apply to PhD' page for current deadlines (applications considered in admissions committee meetings).
notes: Support covers tuition, stipend and travel; students receive research assistantships; full terms in Graduate School policies and student support pages.

title: Austrian Government / OeAD scholarships (Ernst Mach, Franz Werfel, Erhard Busek etc.)
overview: The OeAD (Austrian Agency for Education and Internationalisation) administers several scholarship programmes (Ernst Mach, Franz Werfel, Erhard Busek, Ernst Mach grants) for students, researchers and lecturers to study or conduct research in Austria.
fundingDetails: OeAD programmes provide monthly scholarships (amounts vary by programme); general guidance: monthly costs and ranges and grant durations are published per programme on OeAD and grants.at database. See official OeAD overview: https://oead.at/en/study-research-teaching/overview-grants-and-scholarships
countries: Host country: Austria. Many OeAD programmes are open worldwide
institutions: Example Austrian host universities: University of Vienna; Vienna University of Technology; Graz University of Technology; University of Innsbruck; University of Salzburg; University of Graz. OeAD programmes list and grants database: https://oead.at/en/ and https://grants.at/
requirements: Representative excerpt (OeAD 'Scholarship conditions'): "The scholarship is intended solely for the purpose of carrying out the study or research project at the institution stated in the scholarship award..." Full programme conditions and application steps available via grants.at and OeAD programme pages.
applicationCycle: Opening/closing dates differ per programme; OeAD publishes calls and deadlines: https://oead.at/de/aktuelles/artikel/2025/11/oead-scholarship-programmes-and-further-grants-to-austria-for-2026-2027 and grants.at online application tool.
notes: Terms vary by programme; OeAD provides accommodation assistance and guidance on extension procedures; full scholarship conditions available on OeAD pages.

title: Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung (KAS) Scholarship (Germany)
overview: KAS supports international students and doctoral candidates from non-EU countries through its International Scholarship Programme, awarding scholarships to academically strong, socially engaged candidates with interest in political and civic participation.
fundingDetails: KAS official page states: master's/postgraduate recipients can receive up to €992 per month; doctoral candidates up to €1,400 per month (figures published on KAS site).
countries: Host country: Germany; applicants are generally from non-EU countries; specific regional offices manage applications.
institutions: KAS scholars study across German higher education institutions. Example universities: Humboldt University Berlin; Free University Berlin; University of Cologne; University of Bonn; University of Tübingen; University of Mannheim. KAS International Talent Development info: https://www.kas.de/en/web/begabtenfoerderung-und-kultur/international-talent-development
requirements: Representative excerpt (KAS Guidelines PDF): "When applying for a scholarship, the following documents in triplicate should be submitted... a detailed CV... certified university certificates... four passport photographs..." Full Guidelines PDF: https://www.kas.de/c/document_library/get_file?groupId=252038&uuid=3fa4ff42-43ce-cbe5-6226-884c25309f22
applicationCycle: Applications handled by KAS foreign offices or the KAS central office; deadlines vary by country — consult KAS country office pages or central KAS Intl Talent Development page.
notes: KAS requires social/political engagement and language requirements (often German B2). Scholarship durations: Master's typically two years; doctoral up to three years; additional allowances (family, health) may apply per country office.

title: Joint Japan / World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program
provider: World Bank (funded by Government of Japan)
overview: World Bank (funded by Government of Japan)
fundingDetails: Tuition & fees, monthly subsistence allowance, round-trip airfare, health insurance, travel allowance (per-call amounts vary)
countries: Participating universities worldwide
eligibleApplicants: Nationals of eligible World Bank member developing countries; mid-career professionals
institutions: Columbia University (USA); University of Manchester (UK); University of Tokyo (Japan); University of Cape Town (SA); Lund University (Sweden); University of Birmingham (UK)
requirements: Applicants must be nationals of World Bank member developing country; hold a bachelor’s degree; have ≥2 years development-related work experience; be in good health; not hold dual citizenship with a developed country.
applicationCycle: Published per-call on World Bank JJ/WBGSP pages (varies).
notes: Recipients expected to return to home country; number of awards varies per call.

title: JASSO — Student Exchange Support / Scholarships for Study in Japan
overview: Japan Student Services Organization (JASSO)
fundingDetails: Typically monthly stipends; amounts depend on scheme and year
countries: Japan
eligibleApplicants: International students accepted by Japanese universities under exchange or study agreements
level: Exchange / Study (Undergrad, Graduate depending on scheme)
institutions: University of Tokyo; Kyoto University; Osaka University; Tohoku University; Nagoya University; Keio University
requirements: JASSO offers scholarships for qualified international students accepted by a Japanese university under a student exchange agreement.
applicationCycle: University/host-institution specific (varies by university/constituency).
notes: Recipients must register full-time and maintain academic progress.

title: Netherlands Fellowship Programmes (NFP)
overview: NUFFIC (Dutch government-managed)
fundingDetails: Typically tuition, travel, living allowance, insurance (varies by call)
countries: Netherlands
eligibleApplicants: Mid-career professionals from partner/target countries listed per-call
institutions: Erasmus Univ Rotterdam; Wageningen University & Research; Delft University of Technology; University of Groningen; Leiden University; Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam
requirements: Programmes are designed to match the Dutch government's goals for development aid and education.
applicationCycle: Published per-call; may be constituency/country specific.
notes: Often require return-to-service obligations; durations depend on fellowship.

title: IDRC — Doctoral Research Awards (IDRA) / Research Awards
overview: International Development Research Centre (Canada)
fundingDetails: Merit-based research funding amounts specified in each call (varies)
countries: Canada (enrolled students at Canadian universities conducting field research abroad)
eligibleApplicants: Doctoral students enrolled at Canadian universities conducting field research in developing countries
institutions: University of Toronto; University of British Columbia; McGill University; Université de Montréal; University of Ottawa; University of Alberta
requirements: Short-listed candidates will be asked to provide official transcripts, confirmation of registration in a master’s or doctoral program, recommendation forms.
applicationCycle: Calls published periodically on IDRC funding pages.
notes: Awards support field research in developing countries and include reporting requirements.

title: Mandela Rhodes Scholarship
overview: Mandela Rhodes Foundation
fundingDetails: Tuition fees, accommodation, living stipend, leadership development programme (amounts vary)
countries: South Africa
eligibleApplicants: Citizens of African countries
institutions: University of Cape Town; University of the Witwatersrand; Stellenbosch University; University of Pretoria; Rhodes University; University of KwaZulu-Natal
requirements: Read eligibility criteria; complete online application; provide certified transcripts; two reference letters; personal statement; CV; certified ID.
applicationCycle: Typically opens annually; deadlines on Mandela Rhodes call pages.
notes: Includes leadership programme and alumni engagement; competitive.

title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
overview: Mastercard Foundation (implemented via partner universities)
fundingDetails: Tuition, accommodation, living stipend, mentorship (varies by partner)
countries: Varies by partner university (Canada, USA, Africa, UK, etc.)
eligibleApplicants: Economically disadvantaged young people, primarily in Africa; eligibility set by partner institutions
institutions: University of British Columbia; Ashesi University; KNUST; University of Cape Town; Arizona State University; University of Toronto
requirements: Application process and decisions are managed by each partner university.
applicationCycle: Partner-institution specific.
notes: Terms and renewal conditions vary by partner; often include mentorship and career development.

title: France Excellence Charpak Scholarships
overview: Campus France (France Excellence Charpak)
fundingDetails: Monthly allowance (examples ~€700/month), tuition fee contribution up to €5,000 (varies by call/sub-programme)
countries: France
eligibleApplicants: Target countries vary by Charpak sub-programme (e.g., India-focused streams)
institutions: Université Paris-Saclay; Sciences Po; Université de Lyon; Sorbonne University; Université Grenoble Alpes; Université de Bordeaux
requirements: Designed for one to two years of studies in France at the Master's degree level.
applicationCycle: Varies by sub-programme and year; see Campus France pages.
notes: Target countries and benefits vary by stream.

title: Islamic Development Bank Scholarship Programs
overview: Islamic Development Bank (IsDB)
fundingDetails: Tuition, living allowance, travel, medical insurance (varies by programme)
countries: Partner universities worldwide (varies by program)
eligibleApplicants: Nationals of IsDB Member Countries (including LDMCs)
level: Undergraduate, Master's, MSc (LDMCs) depending on programme
institutions: Universities in Malaysia, Turkey, Indonesia and other partner countries (various)
requirements: Provide educational opportunities to financially impoverished but academically successful young men and women from LDMCs.
applicationCycle: Announced annually; see IsDB scholarship booklets and call materials.
notes: Service obligations and durations vary by programme.

title: GREAT Scholarships
provider: British Council / UK Government (GREAT campaign) co-funded with UK universities
overview: British Council / UK Government (GREAT campaign) co-funded with UK universities
fundingDetails: Typically tuition-fee contribution (~£10,000 historically) (varies by year and country)
countries: United Kingdom
eligibleApplicants: Nationals of selected partner countries (list varies by year)
institutions: University of Manchester; University of Edinburgh; King's College London; University of Sheffield; University of Glasgow; Queen Mary University of London
requirements: GREAT Scholarships offer students from selected countries the opportunity to have £10,000 towards tuition fees for one-year taught postgraduate courses.
applicationCycle: Per-country and per-university (constituency-specific).
notes: Co-funded by partner universities; number of awards varies.

title: DAAD EPOS — Development-Related Postgraduate Courses
overview: DAAD (German Academic Exchange Service)
fundingDetails: Monthly stipend, travel allowance, health insurance, tuition coverage (exact rates in call docs)
countries: Germany
eligibleApplicants: Participants from developing countries listed in EPOS guidelines
institutions: Technical University of Munich; University of Bonn; University of Göttingen; Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin; University of Freiburg; University of Cologne
requirements: EPOS offers individual scholarships to participants from developing countries to study development-related postgraduate courses at selected universities in Germany.
applicationCycle: Published annually and may be country-specific; check DAAD country offices.
notes: Scholars expected to apply skills to home country development; duration corresponds to course length.

title: OPEC Fund for International Development Scholarship Award
overview: OPEC Fund for International Development
fundingDetails: Tuition, living allowance, travel expenses (varies by call)
countries: Depends on partner institution (varies)
eligibleApplicants: Outstanding students from OFID-eligible developing countries
institutions: University of Oxford; University of Manchester; University of Glasgow; AUB; University of the West Indies; University of Cape Town
requirements: Supports outstanding young individuals from developing countries to pursue graduate studies.
applicationCycle: Published with each scholarship call on OFID scholarship page.
notes: Terms and obligations published in call documents.

title: Yenching Academy Fellowship
overview: Peking University
fundingDetails: Tuition, accommodation, monthly stipend, research travel, insurance (exact amounts in admissions materials)
countries: China (Beijing)
eligibleApplicants: International applicants worldwide
level: Two-year interdisciplinary Master's in China Studies
institutions: Peking University; cooperating partners include Harvard, Oxford, Beijing Normal University, University of Tokyo, NUS, ANU
requirements: Yenching Academy only accepts official transcripts; pre-admitted candidates must arrange official transcripts etc.
applicationCycle: Annual application windows published on Yenching admissions pages.
notes: Two-year programme; recipients must reside on campus first year; benefits and renewal in admissions pages.

title: Skoll Scholarship (Oxford Saïd MBA)
provider: Skoll Centre / Saïd Business School, University of Oxford
overview: Skoll Centre / Saïd Business School, University of Oxford
fundingDetails: Full MBA tuition and partial living expenses (historically discretionary support up to a cap)
countries: United Kingdom (University of Oxford)
eligibleApplicants: Applicants to Oxford MBA who are social entrepreneurs meeting eligibility
institutions: Saïd Business School, University of Oxford
requirements: Provides funding for social entrepreneurs; covers full MBA fees and partial living expenses.
applicationCycle: Aligns with Oxford MBA admissions rounds.
notes: Number of Skoll Scholars limited per year; recipients must matriculate in the Oxford MBA program.

title: Weidenfeld-Hoffmann Scholarships & Leadership Programme
overview: Weidenfeld-Hoffmann Trust & University of Oxford
fundingDetails: Fully funded — course fees, living stipend, travel, other allowances as specified
countries: United Kingdom (University of Oxford)
eligibleApplicants: Outstanding students from developing and emerging economies (see Trust lists)
institutions: Blavatnik School of Government; Saïd Business School; Faculty of Law; Department of Economics; Computer Science; Department of International Development
requirements: Applications must be submitted by January deadline to be considered for Weidenfeld-Hoffmann scholarships.
applicationCycle: Published annually; departmental deadlines (often January).
notes: Includes leadership training and mentoring; renewal depends on academic progress.

title: Four-Year Doctoral Fellowship (4YF) — UBC
overview: University of British Columbia (UBC)
fundingDetails: Financial support of at least CAD $18,200 per year plus tuition for up to four years (as published)
countries: Canada (UBC)
eligibleApplicants: Domestic and international doctoral students admitted to UBC
institutions: Faculty of Science; Applied Science; Sauder School of Business; Arts; Medicine; Forestry
requirements: 4YF ensures top doctoral students receive financial support of at least $18,200 per year plus tuition for up to four years.
applicationCycle: Allocated annually by faculties and graduate programs; check UBC Grad School pages.
notes: Multi-year commitment; eligibility, renewal and hold policies provided in guidelines.

title: Project for Human Resource Development (JDS)
overview: Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA)
fundingDetails: Fully funded: tuition, round-trip economy travel, monthly living allowance, medical insurance, settling-in allowances (varies by country call)
countries: Japan
eligibleApplicants: Mid-career government officials from selected partner countries
institutions: University of Tokyo; Waseda University; Kyoto University; Keio University; Osaka University; Nagoya University
requirements: JDS fellows expected to contribute to socio-economic development and return to public service after completion.
applicationCycle: Country-specific application windows published by JDS/JICA country offices.
notes: Strong return-to-service expectation; terms vary by country call.

title: Fundación Carolina Scholarships
overview: Fundación Carolina (Spain)
fundingDetails: Tuition coverage, monthly stipend, travel grants, health insurance (varies by call)
countries: Spain
eligibleApplicants: Primarily Ibero-American candidates (see call documents)
institutions: Universidad Complutense de Madrid; Universidad Autónoma de Madrid; Universidad de Salamanca; Universitat de Barcelona; Universidad Carlos III; Universidad de Valencia
requirements: Full information on requirements and conditions is available on the official Fundación Carolina website.
applicationCycle: Annual call with exact dates published on website.
notes: Competitive; terms vary by call.

title: Cambridge Trust – Cambridge International Scholarships & Trust Awards
overview: Cambridge Trust (University of Cambridge)
fundingDetails: Part-cost to full-cost awards covering tuition and maintenance (values vary)
countries: United Kingdom (University of Cambridge)
eligibleApplicants: International (non-UK/Ireland) applicants meeting scholarship criteria
institutions: Trinity College; St John's College; Department of Engineering; Department of Physics; Judge Business School; Faculty of Law
requirements: Offered to highly-ranked applicants from countries other than the UK or Ireland.
applicationCycle: Varies by department and scholarship; check Cambridge Trust & University pages.
notes: Terms depend on specific scholarship and university regulations.

title: LPDP (Indonesia Endowment Fund for Education)
overview: Indonesia Endowment Fund for Education (LPDP)
fundingDetails: Full tuition, monthly living allowance, book allowance, round-trip airfare (per policy documents)
countries: Indonesia and approved overseas universities
eligibleApplicants: Indonesian citizens
institutions: University of Oxford; University of Cambridge; Harvard University; University of Melbourne; Seoul National University; NUS
requirements: Register online on the LPDP Scholarship Registration site; complete and upload required documents as per the call.
applicationCycle: Registration windows and selection schedules per cohort annually on LPDP site.
notes: Recipients typically have return-to-service obligations in Indonesia.

title: CONACYT Scholarships
provider: Consejo Nacional de Ciencia y Tecnología (Mexico)
overview: Consejo Nacional de Ciencia y Tecnología (Mexico)
fundingDetails: Tuition, stipends, research support (varies by program/year)
countries: Mexico and approved foreign institutions (depending on program)
eligibleApplicants: Mexican nationals meeting call criteria
institutions: UNAM; Tecnológico de Monterrey; Cinvestav; IPN; Universidad Autónoma de Nuevo León; Universidad de Guadalajara
requirements: Aimed at persons of Mexican nationality admitted or enrolled in postgraduate programs.
applicationCycle: Calls and deadlines published on official site; vary by program and year.
notes: Includes reporting requirements and must follow Mexico's research and training policies.

title: OAS — Academic Scholarship Program (Graduate)
overview: Organization of American States (OAS)
fundingDetails: Grants up to US$10,000 per academic year for certain categories; other allowances depend on category
countries: OAS Member States (regional universities)
eligibleApplicants: Citizens of OAS member states
institutions: University of the West Indies; University of Sao Paulo; UNAM; University of Buenos Aires; Pontifical Catholic University of Chile
requirements: Academic scholarships for study or research leading to a degree in an OAS Member State.
applicationCycle: Published annually (example deadlines in March for next academic year).
notes: Award durations vary by category; see call documents.

title: Inter-American Development Bank — Master Degree Scholarship programs
overview: Inter-American Development Bank (IDB)
fundingDetails: Tuition, insurance, living expenses, round-trip airfare (varies by project/call)
countries: Partner institutions in Latin America and beyond (varies by program)
eligibleApplicants: Often public professionals from borrowing member countries
institutions: University of Chile; Harvard Kennedy School (partnerships); FLACSO; Universidad de los Andes; INCAE; Universidad de Costa Rica
requirements: Program for scholarships for public professionals; Bank may finance students for each promotion.
applicationCycle: Published on IDB project/program pages.
notes: Many target public sector professionals and include return-to-service expectations.

title: Shanghai Government Scholarship
overview: Shanghai Municipal Education Commission
fundingDetails: Type A (Full) and Type B (Partial): tuition, accommodation, medical insurance; exact details in guide
countries: China (Shanghai)
eligibleApplicants: International students (non-Chinese citizens) meeting requirements
institutions: Fudan University; Shanghai Jiao Tong University; Tongji University; ECNU; Shanghai University; ShanghaiTech University
requirements: Applicant must be a non-Chinese citizen in good health; age and education background requirements apply.
applicationCycle: Published annually on Shanghai education portals.
notes: Type A = full coverage; Type B = partial; eligibility and age criteria apply.

title: Tianjin Government Scholarship
overview: Tianjin Municipal Education Commission
fundingDetails: Tuition waivers and living-cost support for one-year periods (renewable); values vary
countries: China (Tianjin)
eligibleApplicants: Foreign citizens meeting degree/age/health criteria
institutions: Tianjin University; Nankai University; Tianjin University of Science & Technology; Tianjin Medical University; Tianjin Normal University; Tianjin University of Technology
requirements: Applicants must be foreign citizens and in good health; winners enjoy tuition fee exemption for the first year.
applicationCycle: Published annually by Tianjin Municipal Education Commission and host universities.
notes: Usually one-year scholarships (renewable subject to conditions).

title: Open Doors (Russian Government Scholarship Project)
provider: Open Doors offers tuition-free scholarships via an Olympiad selection process to international students for degree study at 24 leading Russian universities.
overview: Open Doors offers tuition-free scholarships via an Olympiad selection process to international students for degree study at 24 leading Russian universities.
fundingDetails: Register for the Open Doors Olympiad; pass multi-stage selection (portfolio, subject tests, interviews); transcripts; motivation letter; language proof as required.
countries: Tuition waiver; state-funded living stipend; dormitory accommodation; health insurance; social benefits; possible travel allowances for winners.
eligibleApplicants: Russia (participating universities) – applicants from many countries eligible.
level: Bauman MSTU; Sechenov First Moscow State Medical University; HSE University; ITMO University; MIPT; MGIMO; Novosibirsk State University; Tomsk State University.
institutions: Annual – registration and Olympiad stages typically run Sept–Nov (e.g., registration 1 Sep–16 Nov 2025).
requirements: https://od.globaluni.ru/
applicationCycle: Open Doors registration (example) 1 Sep–16 Nov 2025; subsequent university enrolment follows Olympiad results.

title: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships
provider: New Zealand government scholarships for citizens of partner developing countries to study postgraduate programs in New Zealand.
overview: New Zealand government scholarships for citizens of partner developing countries to study postgraduate programs in New Zealand.
fundingDetails: Relevant bachelor’s degree; often 2 years relevant work experience; transcripts; CV; references; proof of English proficiency; evidence of leadership/development impact.
countries: Full tuition; living allowance; establishment allowance; travel grants (economy return airfare); health insurance.
eligibleApplicants: New Zealand (at New Zealand universities/institutions).
level: University of Auckland; University of Otago; Victoria University of Wellington; University of Canterbury; Massey University; University of Waikato.
institutions: Annual – example window 1–31 March 2026 for 2026 intake (country-specific windows apply).
requirements: https://www.manakaiaotearoa.nz/scholarships
applicationCycle: University admission deadlines often earlier (e.g., University of Auckland Semester 1 postgraduate applications often due by Dec 1 prior to intake).

title: Asian Development Bank – Japan Scholarship Program (ADB-JSP)
overview: ADB–Japan Scholarship Program (JSP) funds postgraduate studies (usually master's level) in development-related fields at selected institutions in Asia and the Pacific; targeted at nationals of ADB borrowing member countries to build capacity for development.
fundingDetails: funding components vary by host university; official program page lists covered items (tuition, living allowance, travel, etc.). See official details: https://www.adb.org/work-with-us/careers/japan-scholarship-program
countries: Nationals of ADB borrowing member countries  https://www.adb.org/work-with-us/careers/japan-scholarship-program . Host institutions across Asia-Pacific.
institutions: Examples: Asian Institute of Technology (AIT) - MPA/MPM; University of the Philippines - M.A. Development Studies; University of Malaya - M.Sc.; Chiang Mai University - M.Sc.; Thammasat University - M.Sc.; Institut Teknologi Bandung - M.Sc. | Authoritative listing: https://www.adb.org/work-with-us/careers/japan-scholarship-program
requirements: "The candidate should: be a national of an ADB borrowing member country and Japanese ODA scholarship-eligible country; not be older than 35 years; be in good health; hold a bachelor degree or its equivalent with superior academic record." Full procedures: https://www.adb.org/work-with-us/careers/japan-scholarship-program/procedures-applying
applicationCycle: Application cycle: constituency-specific —
notes: Additional: Coverage normally includes tuition, subsistence allowance, travel, insurance; varies by host. Return-to-home-country expectations noted in program guidance.

title: Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation Scholarships (India)
overview: Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation awards scholarships to outstanding Indian students for postgraduate study abroad at top institutions worldwide; merit-based and highly selective.
fundingDetails: benefit packages determined by the Foundation per award. See official pages: https://inlaksfoundation.org/opportunities/scholarship/
countries: Indian nationals (must be Indian passport holders and resident in India at application time).
institutions: Examples where Inlaks scholars have studied: Columbia University; Oxford University; LSE; Yale University; University of Toronto; Sciences Po. Official program page: https://inlaksfoundation.org/opportunities/scholarship/
requirements: "Applicants must be Indian passport holders and must be resident in India at the time of application... The Applicant must have the following required minimum percentage/grade in both Bachelors and Masters degree (if applicable)" — full eligibility and portal: https://inlaksfoundation.org/opportunities/scholarship/
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual; call typically opens in March — see Inlaks official page and online application portal for exact dates: https://inlaksfoundation.org/opportunities/scholarship/ and https://onlineapplication.inlaksfoundation.org/
notes: Additional: Highly selective; renewability and terms at Foundation discretion; successful applicants subject to Foundation conditions.

title: KAIST Global Scholarship / KAIST Graduate Scholarship (Korea)
overview: KAIST offers graduate scholarships (tuition waivers, school support fees, and in many cases stipends) to international master's and PhD students; awards are tied to admission and academic performance.
fundingDetails: Partial published components: scholarship corresponding to school support fees for 1 year (2 semesters) at admission; further stipend/welfare details published per school. See: https://www.kaist.ac.kr/en/html/edu/03100502.html
countries: Host country: Republic of Korea (KAIST campus, Daejeon). Eligible to admitted international students.
institutions: Examples: KAIST Schools/Departments include Electrical Engineering; Mechanical Engineering; Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering; Computer Science; Business (MBA); Public Policy. Scholarship pages: https://www.kaist.ac.kr/en/html/edu/03100502.html
requirements: "Provide a scholarship corresponding to school support fees for 1 year (2 semesters) after admission regardless of academic record... Provide a scholarship corresponding to school support fees if the student has completed at least 12 credits in the previous semester and maintained a cumulative GPA of B- (3.0/4.3)." Full KAIST pages: https://www.kaist.ac.kr/en/html/edu/03100502.html
applicationCycle: Application cycle: scholarship consideration tied to KAIST admission cycles (semester intake deadlines vary by department).
notes: Additional: Renewal requires minimum GPA and credit requirements; some scholarships include monthly allowance and insurance; amounts vary by department.

title: Heinrich-Böll-Foundation Scholarships (Germany)
overview: Heinrich-Böll-Foundation funds international and German students for undergraduate, graduate and doctoral study who demonstrate academic excellence and socio-political engagement in line with the Foundation's values.
fundingDetails: foundation provides monthly grants and support; detailed amounts and family allowances are specified to awardees; core program page: https://www.boell.de/en/scholarships
countries: Host country: Germany; supports studies in Germany and in some cases study abroad.
institutions: Examples of universities where scholars study: Free University of Berlin; Humboldt University; Technical University of Munich; University of Cologne; University of Leipzig; University of Göttingen. Official info: https://www.boell.de/en/scholarships
requirements: "We accept only online applications. 1. Gather information on the application process and the requested documents. 2. Register for the online application portal. 3. Provide the requested information in the Online Application Portal. 4. After uploading all of your documents, submit your application before the end of the application deadline." See full application instructions: https://www.boell.de/en/application
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual — deadlines and country-specific rounds published on Foundation country pages and application portal.
notes: Additional: Priority for applicants from DAC countries; selection entails written application and interviews; support often covers full degree and includes networking/seminar obligations.

title: AECID / MAEC Spanish Government Scholarships (Spain)
overview: MAEC-AECID scholarships are Spanish government-funded fellowships for candidates from partner countries to pursue Master’s, PhD, research and training in Spain under cooperation and diplomatic channels.
fundingDetails: Funding: components (monthly maintenance, tuition contribution, travel, insurance) are specified in each annual call; if amounts not centrally posted:  — see call notices (example embassy call): https://www.exteriores.gob.es/Embajadas/abuja/en/Comunicacion/Noticias/Paginas/Articulos/Call-for-MAEC-AECID-Scholarships-20252026-from-its-Diplomatic-School-Programme.aspx
countries: Eligible partner countries vary by call (often Latin America, Africa, Asia partner countries). Host country: Spain.
institutions: Examples of host institutions: Universidad Complutense de Madrid; Universidad Autónoma de Madrid; University of Barcelona; Universidad de Salamanca; IE University; Universidad Carlos III. Call pages and lists: https://www.aecid.es/scholarships/
requirements: "The Spanish Agency for International Development Cooperation (AECID) has opened the call for applications for the MAEC-AECID 2025/2026 scholarships..." — see the official call PDF/page for the full checklist.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual — call publication and specific deadlines on AECID central pages and embassy notices (constituency-specific for nominations).
notes: Additional: Specific nationality and residence eligibility, language and admission preconditions; travel & insurance terms set in call documents.

title: Orange Knowledge Programme (OKP) — Netherlands
overview: Orange Knowledge Programme (OKP) (Nuffic) funds scholarships for mid-career professionals and capacity-building for development by financing Master's and short programmes in the Netherlands and partner countries.
fundingDetails: components (tuition, subsistence, travel, visa/insurance) vary by participating institution and call; see Nuffic/OKP pages: https://www.nuffic.nl/en/international/international-programmes/individual-scholarships-orange-knowledge-programme
countries: Eligible list of partner countries (typically 55) — see Nuffic page for authoritative country list.
institutions: Examples of participating Dutch hosts: Wageningen University & Research; University of Amsterdam; Delft University of Technology; Leiden University; Erasmus University Rotterdam; Maastricht University.
requirements: "The Orange Knowledge Programme gave Dutch education institutions the opportunity to offer scholarships to mid-career professionals from 55 countries..." Full call info and institution participation on Nuffic pages.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: rounds published annually
notes: Additional: Often targets mid-career professionals with minimum work experience; some courses require employer nomination; co-financing may apply.

title: University of British Columbia (UBC) International Tuition Awards
overview: UBC International Tuition Awards assist eligible international graduate students registered full-time in research-oriented Master's and doctoral programs with tuition assistance.
fundingDetails: centrally — award amounts are program-specific and displayed on individual faculty/program pages; see central description: https://www.grad.ubc.ca/awards/international-tuition-award
countries: Host country: Canada (UBC Vancouver campus); available to eligible international graduate students at UBC.
institutions: Examples of eligible programs: M.Sc. Computer Science; M.A. Economics; M.Sc. Forestry; PhD Biology; M.Sc. Engineering; M.A. Political Science
requirements: "International Tuition Awards assist international graduate students with their tuition fees if they are registered full-time in research-oriented master's and doctoral programs at UBC - Vancouver campus." Full award guidance: https://www.grad.ubc.ca/awards/international-tuition-award
applicationCycle: Application/assignment cycle: awards assigned to eligible registered students generally before term start; consult Graduate Awards & program offices for exact timelines.
notes: Additional: Award applied to tuition; assignment managed by Graduate Awards; eligibility determined by program designation; contact faculty graduate office for amounts.

title: Yenching Academy Scholarship — Peking University
overview: Yenching Academy offers a fully-funded interdisciplinary master's programme at Peking University (Yenching Master's) focusing on China Studies and global affairs; scholarship covers tuition, accommodation and allowances.
fundingDetails: centrally (components listed but amounts vary or not always published): tuition waiver, on‑campus accommodation, travel stipend and living allowance — see Yenching application details: https://apply.yca.pku.edu.cn/
countries: Host country: China (Peking University, Beijing).
institutions: Programme: Yenching Academy Master’s in China Studies / Global Affairs at Peking University (interdisciplinary programme) — see Yenching Academy site: https://yenchingacademy.org/ and application portal.
requirements: "Yenching Academy only accepts official transcripts. All pre-admitted candidates must either arrange for their university to send official transcripts..." Full instructions available at the Yenching application portal: https://apply.yca.pku.edu.cn/detail?category=AppInstr&id=735055839961812933
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual — Yenching publishes application opening & closing dates on the admissions portal; consult https://apply.yca.pku.edu.cn/ for exact dates.
notes: Additional: Scholars generally required to live on campus in the first year; visa and health insurance per Chinese law and PKU policy; scholarship terms listed on the academy site.

title: Erasmus+ International Credit Mobility (ICM) scholarships
overview: Erasmus+ ICM supports short-term student and staff mobility between EU Programme Countries and Partner Countries via institutional partnerships; grants cover travel, subsistence and (where applicable) contribution to tuition.
fundingDetails: Funding: unit rates and grant components are published in the Erasmus+ ICM handbook and call documents (amounts vary by country group and duration) — see handbook: https://erasmus-plus.ec.europa.eu/document/erasmus-international-credit-mobility-handbook-for-participating-organisations-calls-2024-2025
countries: Countries: Programme Countries (EU/EEA) and Partner Countries as defined per call/handbook.
institutions: Examples of participating HEIs: University of Barcelona; University of Warsaw; University of Porto; University of Birmingham; KU Leuven; University of Oslo
requirements: "International Credit Mobility supports student and staff exchanges between Programme and Partner countries..." — full handbook and financial rates: https://erasmus-plus.ec.europa.eu/document/erasmus-international-credit-mobility-handbook-for-participating-organisations-calls-2024-2025
applicationCycle: Application cycle: Calls are published annually (institution-led applications)
notes: Additional: Mobility applications are submitted by institutions; quotas and grant rates per partnership; visa/insurance responsibilities as in the ICM handbook.

title: University of Tokyo — G30 / MEXT-backed Scholarships (Japan)
overview: UTokyo participates in Global 30 programmes and accepts MEXT scholarship awardees; MEXT provides Japanese Government scholarships for international students for study in Japan and UTokyo provides additional internal scholarships.
fundingDetails: centrally here — MEXT publishes scholarship categories (tuition waiver, monthly stipend, travel) on MEXT and UTokyo pages — see: https://www.u-tokyo.ac.jp/en/prospective-students/mext_scholarship.html
countries: Host country: Japan (University of Tokyo).
institutions: Participating programmes: UTokyo Graduate Schools (Engineering, Science, Agricultural & Life Sciences, Interfaculty, etc.); Global 30 scholarship listings: https://global30.t.u-tokyo.ac.jp/g30_hp/scholarships.html
requirements: "This is a program through which students are able to study at a Japanese university with a scholarship from the Japanese government."
applicationCycle: Application cycle: MEXT scholarship has annual application windows (embassy and university routes); constituency-specific deadlines for embassy nominations
notes: Additional: MEXT conditions include age limits, health, nationality rules; compulsory pre‑study Japanese language course sometimes provided; further UTokyo internal award terms apply.

title: Gulbenkian Foundation / Calouste Gulbenkian Scholarships
overview: Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation offers a range of scholarships and research grants for study in Portugal and internationally, including merit and discipline-specific awards for postgraduate study and research.
fundingDetails: Some calls publish amounts — example: certain higher education scholarships provide a fixed annual grant of €4,000 See master list: https://gulbenkian.pt/en/bolsas-lista/
countries: Host country: Portugal (and specific international fellowships depending on call).
institutions: Examples of host institutions: Universidade de Lisboa; Universidade NOVA de Lisboa; Universidade do Porto; Universidade de Coimbra; ISCTE; Católica Lisbon. Gulbenkian scholarship list: https://gulbenkian.pt/en/bolsas-lista/
requirements: "Up to 50 new scholarships are awarded each year, for a maximum duration of three years. Each scholarship provides a fixed annual grant of €4,000
applicationCycle: Application cycle: varies by scholarship type — Gulbenkian publishes call calendars and opens applications per call
notes: Additional: Terms, duration and number of awards vary by call; some are limited to research on Portuguese culture; full conditions on call pages.

title: Sweden — Adlerbert Study Scholarships / Specific Swedish university scholarships
overview: Adlerbert Foundations fund scholarships administered by Swedish universities (e.g., Chalmers, University of Gothenburg) offering travel grants, living support or tuition waivers for selected international students and visiting researchers.
fundingDetails: Example published component (University of Gothenburg): SEK 15,000 for study trips or living expenses; other Adlerbert-funded tuition waivers published on host pages (amounts vary). See host pages: https://studentportal.gu.se/en/news/adlerbert-scholarships-2026 and Chalmers Adlerbert page.
countries: Host country: Sweden (participating Swedish universities such as Chalmers, University of Gothenburg).
institutions: Examples of participating hosts: Chalmers University of Technology; University of Gothenburg; Lund University (select scholarships); KTH Royal Institute of Technology; Uppsala University; Linköping University — see host pages for full lists.
requirements: "You can apply for a scholarship of SEK 15,000 for either study trips or living expenses during your studies, with priority given to trips." See host university pages for full checklist and application details.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: varies by host university — check host pages for exact opening & closing dates (example Gothenburg publication lists application windows).
notes: Additional: Coverage and eligibility vary; some awards are one-off travel/living grants while others may be full tuition waivers; terms and renewal rules set by host institutions.

title: Belgium – ARES (Cooperation and Development) Master’s Scholarships
overview: ARES International Training Scholarships enable candidates from eligible partner countries to pursue one-year advanced Bachelor's/Master's programmes or short continuing education at Wallonia‑Brussels higher-education institutions in Belgium.
fundingDetails: Funding components (tuition, monthly living allowance, travel) are published in yearly call documents on the ARES site; see ARES call pages: https://www.ares-ac.be/en/scholarships
countries: Host country: Belgium (Wallonia‑Brussels Federation institutions); eligible partner countries per call.
institutions: Examples of participating HEIs: UCLouvain; Université Libre de Bruxelles (ULB); Université de Liège (ULiège); Haute École de la Province de Liège; Institut Supérieur de Promotion Sociale; ISCED programs — see ARES call list.
requirements: "The ARES scholarships offer you the opportunity to follow a one-year advanced bachelor's or master's degree program or a 2-to-6-month continuing education..." Full call documents posted on ARES site.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual calls (see ARES call page for exact dates) — example: 'International training scholarships 2026-2027' call page.
notes: Additional: Eligibility often requires residence/employment in eligible countries; quotas and selection per country; visa and insurance obligations specified in call documents.

title: France — École Normale Supérieure (ENS) International Masters & Doctoral Scholarships
overview: ENS offers international selection routes and scholarships to admit outstanding international candidates to Master's and Doctoral programmes; selected students may receive scholarships/stipends depending on programme.
fundingDetails: Funding: specific ENS scholarships publish amounts (e.g., Ampère Scholarship €1,000/month for one academic year for select programmes) — see ENS pages for programme-specific published amounts: https://www.ens.psl.eu/en/une-formation-d-exception/admissions/international/international-selection
countries: Host country: France (ENS Paris, ENS Lyon and other ENS campuses).
institutions: Participating programmes include ENS master's tracks in Sciences and Arts & Humanities across the PSL network and partner universities; see ENS international selection pages for lists and programme links.
requirements: "International selection in Arts&Humanities 2026: Application : October 1 to December 3, 2025 (12.00 (midday), Paris time)" — see ENS pages for full checklist and selection steps.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual windows published by ENS (example Oct–Dec for international selection).
notes: Additional: Selection involves written tests and oral exams; scholarship renewal and stipends depend on programme and performance; visa/insurance per French regulations.

title: South Africa — NRF (National Research Foundation) International Scholarship / University doctoral packages
overview: The NRF provides postgraduate funding (DSTI-NRF postgraduate student funding) for Master's and PhD students at South African universities; university doctoral packages often combine NRF funding with institutional support.
fundingDetails: Funding: bursary amounts and stipend scales are published in NRF call documents and funding policy PDFs (amounts vary by level and year). See NRF call page for published amounts per call: https://www.nrf.ac.za/dsti-nrf-postgraduate-student-funding-for-the-2026-academic-year/
countries: Host country: South Africa (South African universities).
institutions: Participating institutions: University of Cape Town; University of the Witwatersrand; Stellenbosch University; University of Pretoria; Rhodes University; North-West University. See NRF site for institutional calls and details.
requirements: "The DSTI and NRF are pleased to announce a call for new applications for DSTI-NRF Master's and Doctoral Student Funding for the academic year 2026." Full call documents with application checklists available on NRF website.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual calls with specific opening & closing dates published on NRF site
notes: Additional: Eligibility, renewal, dependants allowances and research grants are specified in NRF call documents and policy PDFs; conditions and reporting obligations apply to awardees.

title: DAAD — Helmut-Schmidt-Programme (Master’s Scholarships, Germany)
overview: The DAAD Helmut-Schmidt-Programme funds master's studies in Germany (Public Policy and Good Governance) for future leaders from developing and emerging countries, including language course support and full degree funding components.
fundingDetails: Published components (DAAD & host pages): monthly stipend (example amounts published on host pages and DAAD info), tuition contribution, health insurance, travel allowance, study and research grant, rent subsidy and family supplement (as applicable). See DAAD page: https://www.daad.de/en/studying-in-germany/scholarships/daad-funding-programmes/helmut-schmidt-programme/
countries: Host country: Germany (participating German HEIs listed on DAAD Helmut-Schmidt programme pages).
institutions: Participating programmes: selected Master's courses in public policy/governance at participating German universities (see DAAD programme listing for partner courses).
requirements: "This programme is designed to further qualify future leaders in politics, law, economics and administration according to the principles of good governance." Full programme info and partner lists on DAAD pages.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: DAAD publishes calls annually — check DAAD Helmut-Schmidt programme page and host university calls for exact opening & closing dates.
notes: Additional: Includes mandatory German language course in Germany (up to 4 months) prior to study, and other benefits; eligibility and renewal conditions per DAAD call.

title: University of Amsterdam — Amsterdam Merit Scholarship (AMS)
overview: The Amsterdam Merit Scholarship (AMS) supports outstanding international (non‑EU/EEA) students applying for UvA Master's and select Bachelor's programmes with financial aid (tuition support) that varies by faculty.
fundingDetails: Published funding: UvA lists amounts and indicates for 2026-2027 an example amount of €25,900 (varies by faculty and year). Faculty-specific AMS pages list exact amounts. See AMS central page: https://www.uva.nl/en/education/fees-and-funding/masters-scholarships-and-loans/amsterdam-merit-scholarship/amsterdam-merit-scholarship.html
countries: Host country: Netherlands (University of Amsterdam).
institutions: Participating programmes/examples: LL.M. programmes at Amsterdam Law School (International & European Law, Law & Finance, International Criminal Law), various MSc and MA programmes across faculties — see AMS page for eligible programmes per faculty.
requirements: "Outstanding students from outside the EU/EEA can apply for an Amsterdam Merit Scholarship (AMS)." Full faculty-specific checklists and amounts are on the AMS page.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual; faculties publish deadlines (example AMS deadlines often align with UvA master application windows, e.g., 15 January or faculty-specific dates).
notes: Additional: Eligibility limited to non‑EU/EEA applicants; number of awards and amounts depend on faculty; recipients must meet visa and enrollment requirements; renewal depends on academic progress.

title: Chevening Scholarships (UK)
overview: Chevening is the UK Government's global scholarship programme funded by the FCDO to support future leaders in pursuing one-year master's degrees at UK universities with full financial support.
fundingDetails: Published funding: Chevening covers tuition fees, a monthly stipend, return economy airfare to the UK, and additional grants/allowances. See official 'what you'll get' page: https://www.chevening.org/scholarships/what-youll-get/
countries: Host country: United Kingdom (eligible UK universities).
institutions: Participating programmes: Any eligible one-year master's course at accredited UK universities (examples include LSE MSc, Oxford MPP, King's College London MA, University of Edinburgh MSc, University of Manchester MSc, University of Bristol MSc).
requirements: "Chevening provides a unique opportunity for future leaders to pursue a one-year master’s degree in the UK. Chevening covers tuition fees, a monthly stipend, travel costs to and from the UK, and other grants and allowances." See Chevening site.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual — opens typically August and closes early November (check country pages for exact dates).
notes: Additional: Applicants must meet nationality, degree and work experience requirements; successful scholars expected to return home for two years after award; visa and health insurance per UK rules.

title: Commonwealth Scholarships (UK)
overview: Commonwealth Scholarships (CSC-funded) support citizens of Commonwealth countries for Master's and PhD study in the UK, prioritizing development impact and capacity building.
fundingDetails: Funding: CSC awards include full tuition, living allowance, approved travel, and other allowances depending on award type; see official CSC pages for exact published allowances per award: https://cscuk.fcdo.gov.uk/learn-more/scholarships/
countries: Host country: United Kingdom (various UK universities).
institutions: Participating programmes: selected development-relevant Master's and PhD programmes at UK universities (examples: University of Sussex, University of Leeds, SOAS, University of Birmingham, University of Glasgow, University of Manchester).
requirements: "Commonwealth Scholarships are for candidates who could not otherwise afford to study in the UK. They enable talented and motivated individuals to gain the knowledge and skills required for sustainable development." Full rules on CSC pages.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: annual calls — country nomination deadlines vary (national nominating agencies publish deadlines).
notes: Additional: Awards usually require home-country nomination; awardees expected to return and contribute to development; visa/insurance per UK regulations; specific allowances detailed in CSC documentation.

title: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degrees (EMJMD) Scholarships
overview: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degrees (EMJMDs) are integrated international programmes delivered by consortia of higher-education institutions; selected students receive EU-funded scholarships covering participation costs, travel and living allowances.
fundingDetails: Published funding: EMJMD scholarships include contribution to participation costs (tuition), monthly allowance, travel contribution and installation costs; exact unit rates are published in the Erasmus+ Programme Guide and on specific EMJMD programme pages.
countries: Countries: mobility across consortium partner countries (EU Programme Countries and partner countries depending on consortium).
institutions: Participating institutions (examples across consortia): University of Groningen, KU Leuven, University of Bologna, University of Edinburgh, Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona, Technical University of Munich — EMJMD catalogue: https://ec.europa.eu/programmes/erasmus-plus/opportunities/individuals/students/erasmus-mundus-joint-master-degrees_en
requirements: "Erasmus Mundus joint master degrees (EMJMDs) are prestigious, integrated, international study programmes... Grants are awarded to individual students selected by the consortia to follow an EMJMD programme." See EU catalogue for full checklists.
applicationCycle: Application cycle: each EMJMD programme sets its own deadlines annually
notes: Additional: Scholarships awarded by consortia; number of scholarships and amounts per cohort specified in programme calls; visa/insurance and residency obligations per host countries.
